review:
I didn't fully understand this game when I first picked it up around its early access release. It sat forgotten in my library for a good year or two after that, gathering dust and honestly, I didn't even think about it. 

When I decided one day to try it out again I was shocked at why I didn't stick with the game the first time around. DRG has been like crack cocaine for me — the gameplay loop, weapons and gear customisation, enemy and mission variety — it has SO much content and SO much going on. 

The best thing by far about this game is its community though. I remember starting as a greenbeard and finding lobbies with veterans who patiently guided me and assisted me for hours on end. Now, after 900 hours I find myself returning the favour to newer players. It doesn't feel like a task or a chore, it brings genuine joy to help the community of a game that has helped me. 

Deep Rock Galactic is likely my favourite game of all time and I highly recommend this to anyone looking for a chill and friendly community and often chaotic missions full of adrenaline and pure fun.

review:
我希望深岩银河这个系列会越做越好

第一次了解到老深岩是我在一次深夜的无意义meme视频里刷到的一个片段：那是一群长满胡子矮人，围着一块黄色琥珀（只是当时认为是琥珀）用着手上的探测器一遍又一遍的喊着“We are rich”。这个片段给了我很大的影响（这是款什么游戏，他们为什么这样做，这个游戏的玩法是什么）抱着这样的心态我去查了一下，这是一款玩法与潜兵相似的多人pve刷宝游戏，我兴致冲冲的打开steam渴望为我贫瘠的游戏库再填一员大将，这是他的价格劝退了我（当时不知道这个游戏dlc只是皮肤）本体加上dlc居然能买到惊人的三位数，这是我当时不能负担的起的，但这个购买的心愿就像一只忐忑的影刀时不时出来划拉我一下。终于在我遇见他第二次降价后，我决定先入手尝尝咸淡，最终我以27块的价格购入了一款我至今都不会腻的游戏。
—————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
我已经不记得我第一次下矿时的狼狈了，但是我记得我第一次在社区发布关于DRG内容时，好多500h+大佬都在我的帖子下评论，他们希望我那一同与他们游玩这款画质可能没那么精美但内容缺不融小觑的游戏；我记得第一次在这个游戏里被大佬夸，那是我一个人单挑无畏后大佬对我的夸赞；我记得第一次打5++虫蛋任务，我因为操作不好倒地很多次，一位红框的枪手大哥一次又一次的把我救起……这款游戏是我至今steam截图数最多的游戏，太多的回忆被定格在了我按下快捷键的那个瞬间，是我第一次进mod房；是我第一次金币数量正好卡到77777；是我第一次被别人夸顶针，是我第一次通关单人5++。这个游戏给我带来的感受太多太多，我说不完，太多的矿工与我并肩作战，我们不认识彼此，可又各自心照不宣，这是我在其他联机游戏里所没体验到的友善，这里没有争吵，没有负能量的情绪输出。是个这社区留住了我，他让我相信互联网只是把人性的恶给放大了，这个世界上还有就像还有霍克斯星这样一块小小星球，存在着一些单纯又友好的矮人。
—————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
晚安鬼船，我希望你们的新作越来越好

晚安英勇无畏的矿工们，我希望你们天天都能在5++里游龙

晚安博斯克，谢谢你陪我度过一个又一个艰难的任务

晚安史蒂夫，记得照顾好自己

晚安朵朵，下次别再把核心岩开开了

晚安茉莉，别再把我挤下去摔死了

哦，对了。管八分钱

review_english: I hope that the Deep Rock Galaxy series will get better and better. The first time I learned about Old Deep Rock was a clip I saw in a meaningless meme video late at night. It was a group of bearded dwarves surrounding a piece of yellow amber. I just thought it was amber at the time and shouted "We are rich" over and over with the detectors on their hands. This clip had a great impact on me. What kind of game is this? Why did they do this? What is the gameplay of this game? With this mentality, I went to check it out. This is a multiplayer PVE treasure farming game with similar gameplay to submarines. I excitedly opened Steam, eager to add another general to my meager game library, but the price persuaded me to give up. I didn’t know at the time that the DLC of this game was just a skin, but the main body plus the DLC could actually be purchased for an astonishing three figures. This was something I couldn’t afford at the time, but this desire to buy was like a nervous shadow knife that came out to scratch me from time to time. Finally, after I met him for the second price reduction, I decided to buy it first to taste it. In the end, I bought a game that I still don’t get tired of for 27 yuan. I can’t remember the embarrassment I felt the first time I entered the mine. But I remember when I first posted about DRG content in the community, many 500h bosses commented on my post. They wanted me to play with them. This game may not have that exquisite graphics but the content is not worth looking down on. I remember the first time I was praised by a boss in this game. It was the boss’s praise of me after I was fearless in a one-on-one challenge. I remember the first time I played the 5 Insect Egg Mission. I fell to the ground many times because of poor operation. A gunman with a red frame rescued me time and time again. This game is the game I have taken the most screenshots on steam so far. Too many memories are fixed in the moment when I pressed the shortcut key. It was my first time entering the mod room. It was my first time that the number of gold coins reached 77777. It was my first time being praised by others. It was my first time clearing Single Player 5. This game gave me so many feelings that I can’t say enough. Too many miners fought side by side with me. We didn’t know each other, but we all understood each other tacitly. This was a friendliness that I had never experienced in other online games. There were no quarrels and no negative emotional output. It was this community that kept me here. It made me believe that the Internet only magnified the evil of human nature. There are still some simple and friendly dwarves in this world, just like Hawks. Good night Ghost Ship. I hope your new work will be better and better. Good night, brave and fearless miners. I hope you can swim in 5 every day. Good night Bosque. Thank you for accompanying me through one difficult task after another. Good night Steve. Remember to take care of yourself. Good night Duoduo. Don’t open the core rock next time. Good night Jasmine. Don’t squeeze me down and kill me. Oh, by the way, it’s eight cents.

review:
I genuinely love Deep Rock Galactic. I’ve put hundreds of hours into it, bought cosmetics, convinced friends to play it, and defended the game for years because it used to feel like one of the few co-op games made by developers who actually cared.

But now it honestly feels abandoned.

Instead of properly supporting the game that built the community, the studio seems more interested in chasing trends with spin-offs like Survivor — which is basically “Vampire Survivors but with dwarves” — and now Rogue Core, which feels like another attempt to turn DRG into a roguelite instead of improving the original game people actually fell in love with.

Meanwhile the base game barely gets meaningful updates anymore. Not even regular balance tweaks, quality-of-life passes, weapon reworks, bugfixes, or hotfixes at the pace you’d expect from a live-service co-op game this popular. There are bugs and issues that sit around forever while development focus clearly goes elsewhere.

And before people spam “the game already has enough content” — that’s not the point. The point is that the original DRG became successful because of the gameplay loop, teamwork, and constant support. Now it feels like that support has slowed to a crawl while the brand gets stretched into side projects.

I’m not even saying Rogue Core or Survivor are terrible games. I’m saying it sucks watching the original game slowly lose momentum while the developers move on to copy-and-paste trend chasing.

DRG deserved better long-term support than this.

Rock and Stone… I guess.

There are bugs and issues that sit around forever while development focus clearly goes elsewhere. And before people spam the game already has enough content that s not the point. The point is that the original DRG became successful because of the gameplay loop, teamwork, and constant support. Now it feels like that support has slowed to a crawl while the brand gets stretched into side projects. I m not even saying Rogue Core or Survivor are terrible games. I m saying it sucks watching the original game slowly lose momentum while the developers move on to copy-and-paste trend chasing. DRG deserved better long-term support than this. Rock and Stone I guess.
review_english: I genuinely love Deep Rock Galactic. I ve put hundreds of hours into it, bought cosmetics, convinced friends to play it, and defended the game for years because it used to feel like one of the few co-op games made by developers who actually cared. But now it honestly feels abandoned. Instead of properly supporting the game that built the community, the studio seems more interested in chasing trends with spin-offs like Survivor which is basically Vampire Survivors but with dwarves and now Rogue Core, which feels like another attempt to turn DRG into a roguelite instead of improving the original game people actually fell in love with. Meanwhile the base game barely gets meaningful updates anymore. review:
From S2 to now
1618 hours in,
I think I’m done with this game — and it’s not because of Rogue Core coming out.
Developers, have you actually played your own game?
(I know you stream the game sometimes… you know what I mean.)
Try soloing Core Corruption on Hazard 5 without BOSCO and without dying.
And preferably without relying on some specific overpowered build.
Why can’t it be designed like the Lithophage Rockpox Corruptor, where players can choose whether or not they want to engage with it?
Back then, Septic Spreaders + Glyphid Stalkers were already pushing it too far.
And now you add Ossiran Scrabs + Pit Jaws?
This is just miserable game design.
Out of the 10 new players I brought into the game, only 1 was willing to keep playing.
Watching this game’s Steam rating go from 99% Overwhelmingly Positive down to 96% honestly just makes me sad.
This game deserved better.

玩了1618小時，

我覺得我玩不下去了——這跟Rogue Core的發布無關。

開發者們，你們真的玩過自己的遊戲嗎？

（我知道你們偶爾會直播……你們懂我的意思。）

試試在Hazard 5難度下單刷Core Corruption(地核石警報)，不用BOSCO，也不用死。
最好不要依賴什麼超強的build。
為什麼不能像Lithophage Rockpox Corruptor(火龍果  S4那隻)那樣設計，讓玩家可以選擇是否與之交戰？
那時候，Septic Spreaders(檳榔，會躲遠程噴紅色的)
和Glyphid Stalkers(隱刀)就已經夠讓人頭痛了。
現在你們又加了Ossiran Scrabs(S6地上爬可飛兩棲的小蟲)和Pit Jaws(隱藏在地上，會把矮人抓到死鍬形蟲)？
這遊戲設計簡直糟糕透頂。
我帶了10個新玩家進來，結果只有1個願意繼續玩下去。
看著這款遊戲在Steam的評分從99%的好評率跌到96%，我真的很難過。
這款遊戲本應得到更好的評價。

review: Que dire... J'ai commencé DRG suite à la vidéo de Kombo sur ce dernier. Le jeu avait l'air bien et la vidéo aussi. J'ai donc lancer ce jeu et quel bonheur. En soit le jeu n'a rien d'exceptionnel sur le papier. Cependant, quand on creuse un peu (sans mauvais jeu de mot) le jeu est bien plus complet qu'au premier abord. Les classes diversifiées, la gestion de ressources, la difficulté de fou quand on lance en danger 5 ou plus, la diversité des missions et les overclocks changent tout le gameplay. Mais ce que je préfère par dessus tout dans ce jeu c'est la communauté. Elle est vraiment ultra saine et bon délire. Voila ce que je pense de ce jeu. Un bon gameplay sublimé par des joueurs exceptionnels. Et je finirait par la phrase qui va vous rester dans la tête pendant on moins 2 jours chaque fois que vous lancerez ce jeu : ROCK AND STONE !!!

review:
Короче, ДРГ - это лучший кооперативный симулятор шахтера-алкоголика, в котором твоя карьерная лестница состоит исключительно из прыжков в темные пещеры и криков во славу камня и камня.

Ты и три твоих бородатых кореша нанимаетесь в самую безжалостную межгалактическую корпорацию, которая отправляет вас на самую опасную планету во вселенной ради добычи блестящих камушков. Сюжет здесь простой: копай золото, стреляй в жуков, не оставляй дворфов позади. Но на деле игра моментально превращается в хаотичный балаган.

Классы персонажей сбалансированы идеально для создания максимального дискомфорта товарищам. Инженер строит платформы в самых неожиданных местах, Скаут улетает на крюке в темноту и расшибается насмерть на другом конце карты, Стрелок ставит зиплайны со скоростью три сантиметра в минуту, а Бурильщик... Бурильщик - это ходячее военное преступление, задача которого - случайно (или нет) подорвать всю команду своей взрывчаткой прямо у входа в эвакуационную капсулу.

Местная фауна - это бесконечные орды гигантских жуков, которые очень не любят, когда у них забирают полезные ископаемые. Но главный враг в этой игре - не жуки, и даже не гравитация, а летающий робот-уборщик по имени Хакси, который упорно отказывается ехать туда, куда надо.

Перед каждой миссией у вас есть важнейший ритуал: прийти в бар на космической станции, заказать у робота-бармена самого крепкого пива, станцевать под джук-бокс, запрыгнуть в капсулу абсолютно пьяными, потерять сознание во время полета и прийти в себя уже в эпицентре роя жуков.

Итог: 10 "ROCK AND STONE!" из 10. Пойду пну бочку в шахту лифта, пока руководство не видит, и закажу еще один раунд «Потла Масляного».

review_english: In short, DRG is the best co-op simulator of an alcoholic miner, in which your career ladder consists solely of jumping into dark caves and screaming for the glory of stone and stone. You and your three bearded buddies are hired by the most ruthless intergalactic corporation, which sends you to the most dangerous planet in the universe in order to mine shiny stones. The plot here is simple: dig for gold, shoot the bugs, don't leave the dwarves behind. But in reality, the game instantly turns into a chaotic farce. Character classes are balanced perfectly to create maximum discomfort for your comrades. The Engineer builds platforms in the most unexpected places, the Scout flies off on a hook into the darkness and falls to death on the other side of the map, the Shooter sets up ziplines at a speed of three centimeters per minute, and the Driller... The Driller is a walking war crime whose task is to accidentally (or not) blow up the entire team with his explosives right at the entrance to the evacuation capsule. The local fauna consists of endless hordes of giant beetles that really don’t like it when minerals are taken from them. But the main enemy in this game is not bugs, or even gravity, but a flying cleaning robot named Huxie, who stubbornly refuses to go where he needs to go. Before each mission, you have the most important ritual of going to a bar on a space station, ordering the strongest beer from the robot bartender, dancing to a jukebox, jumping into a capsule completely drunk, losing consciousness during the flight and coming to your senses in the midst of a swarm of bugs. Result 10 "ROCK AND STONE!" out of 10. I’ll go kick the barrel down the elevator shaft while management isn’t looking, and order another round of Potl Butter.

review: Игра идеальна, для людей, которые любят рогалики. Лично меня зацепила тем, что игровой процесс циркулирует между двумя фазами: пассивной, в которой ты добываешь ресурсы, а так же исследуешь пещеры и активной, в которой стреляешь по врагам. Deep rock отлично дополняет кооперативная составляющая, все классы отлично сбалансированы и у каждого своя задача. Так же не малую роль в кооп-составляющей играет комьюнити - сколько бы раз и от каких бы врагов ты не умирал они всегда поднимут и прикроют. 10 кружек пива из 10

review:
This is a game that you can have a lot of fun on by both playing with friends [b]or[/b] by yourself. There's lots of stuff to do and the progression feels good. [Might feel a little slow at first. But once you get a hang of it, it will feel good.] The learning curve is good, however things might feel a little overwhelming at first. But you'll get up to speed pretty quickly!

I can personally play on Level 3 difficulty with no issues, Level 4 can be pretty challenging playing Solo. Depending on what mission type it is. [There are 5 levels of difficulties. 1-5]
You can also do lots of fun stuff in the mission control, by playing small mini games and drinking beer until you pass out! And the game it self is also pretty goofy!

[h3]Each mission feels different[/h3]
You have quite a few different mission [b]types[/b] and each mission has a secondary goal that will give you some extra bonus points! While some missions could also contain some hidden loot. So keep your eyes peeled!

[h3]In conclusion:[/h3]
I'm having lots of fun playing this game. It's a game that I can kick back, relax, shut my brain off, while keep my focus sharp. And it's not like it's the end of the world if I fail a mission either. So it's a good balance of keeping you focused in the game, but also do stuff without much thinking.

[b]Rock and Stone eveyone![/b]

review:
[b] Плюсы [/b]

- Превосходный кооператив. Игра заточена под командную работу - приятно играть в пати
- Процедурные пещеры. Ощущение новизны и приключения каждый раунд
- Разрушаемая среда и инструменты создают возможности для креатива в подходах к задачам
- Чёткие, разнообразные классы
- Удерживающая прогрессия. Апгрейды и косметика мотивируют возвращаться
- Активная поддержка разработчиков
- Весёлая атмосферная подача и фирменный юмор дварфов.

[b] Минусы [/b]

- Со временем ощущается повторяемость целей и окружения
- Гринд
- Игра теряет часть шарма, если играть в одиночку
- Иногда встречаются технические баги и проблемы с подбором
- Для рандомных команд некоторые миссии будут сложны.

[b] Вывод [/b]

Если вы ищете игру для комфортных вечеров с друзьями, где каждый игрок чувствует свою значимость - это идеальный выбор.
Единственный серьёзный минус (для некоторых) - повторяемость и гринд.

review_english: b Pros b - Excellent co-op. The game is designed for teamwork - it's nice to play in a party - Procedural caves. A sense of newness and adventure every round - Destructible environments and tools create opportunities for creativity in how you approach challenges - Clear, varied classes - Retaining progression. Upgrades and cosmetics motivate you to come back - Active support from developers - Funny atmospheric presentation and signature dwarf humor. b Cons b - Over time, the objectives and environment feel repetitive - Grinding - The game loses some of its charm if you play alone - Sometimes there are technical bugs and problems with selection - For random teams, some missions will be difficult. b Conclusion b If you are looking for a game for comfortable evenings with friends, where each player feels important, this is the ideal choice. The only serious drawback (for some) is the repetition and grind.

review:
[h1]Me and my friends been loving the latest "well crafted" new events the new team have been making on the clearly intended "difficulty" that the devs made![/h1]

Okay I have been holding this review in work for almost three years. 
But every time I opened this page, I feel like I keep getting distracted by new hats, or paints that I kept telling myself I will use. I'm not sure what that says about this game in particular, this ramble is what this game deserves. Rerrr! 

[h1]I would highly recommend this game if you do have 3 spare dwagons near you that you can dognap.[/h1]
[h2]Desc[/h2]
Deep Rock is a very simple game where some company sends you, 4 dwarfs to HOXXES IV to mine and shoot the same bugs. Again. and Again. And then get distracted by some shinies.
(...)
(Also also, if you really like playing alone instead. You can still do that and maybe PET the lootbugs along the way. They are STUPIDLY ROUND and make silly noises. Maybe you can also ask your assistant, Bosco that would at least would NEVER complain about it to help you .. hold Alt-Fire to mine gray rocks. They can also help by staring at you while some ceiling Cave Leech nibbles your muzzle and stuff, owo. So,, Its all worth it. I prolly need to play Solo Haz5+ to amplify the incredibly fun experience. The increased cave complexity +3, infinite insect swarm, and warnings sounds super fun! I love Bosco with its silly "..." voicelines. Mayb its a little shy.

[h2]THINGIES[/h2]
Some do say that the procedural map generation is not exactly exciting. But I do like the consistency of seeing Molley (your personal MULE) gaining the ability to float really makes sense. It keeps the game exciting and full of surprises. I like how many gray rocks there are in HOXXES too, I helped to distract me from noticing the amount of time I got lost in the caves. I would also like to mention how you can bring Doretta head's home with you. What a feature certainly worth mentioning! Now that is the best addition yet since the past whar? 2020?"

While the class and role variety is flexible enough in multiplayer, I think we have to praise how GS has been keeping up with player's need of enemy types. They really truly listened to us for the past few years!

We have bugs that you can headpat and do stuff?
One pulls you and nuke your health through walls, one blinds you. And then, and then, one explodes your whole team I think. I don't remember actually because I'm too busy falling into the same pre-generated holes. The point is, there are too much bugs! My friends kept telling me to watch out for Rockpox and Rival Nemesis or let alone these new Corespawn bugs, or something like that. but then I ask which one is that, before I immediately die anyway.
I believe if they would reinvent the wheel of actually adding new interactable enemy types or actual counterplay to literally anything, me and my friends three collective braincells would prolly die from having to think about them, especially on higher Haz+ and that is not why I clearly play this game for this many hours. Wearh!

By the time I do finish the mission, all I can think about are just grey and brown rocks. I genuinely don't remember anything from the incredibly creative mission types. We have 150 morkite mining, more aquarq mining, and, waiting for asteroid crackers, and OH! more mining but with pipes. I don't know what is there to complain about really. Each mission feels really new to me, thanks to the variety of 4? 6? 7? SIX SEVEN? 9? 10? mission types or something like that my .. paws can't even count that high.  I dunno, I kept forgetting what I did every time a new drop pod lands. I like how Deep Rock respects my time by making everything felt samey so that I don't really have to learn anything new, ever!

I think that the point - Deep rock is onto something by making each mission type felt like a JOB that keeps you around without paying you.

[h2]FEATURES[/h2]
[h3]And then, hear this .. You can press V at any time to yell "Rock and Stone!"[/h3] 
Most importantly, this precedes anything you do. Before a mission. During a Mission? Found Gold? After the host kicks you. Maybe. It is truly a revolutionary turn off your brain to interact with others button.

Yes, I know there is a strong competitor of pinging Gold where your dwarf will say "WE'RE RICH! we're RICH!" But, y'know .. If you went to the Fungus Bogs, there are these giant mushrooms. BUT BUT BUT! When you spam your [X] ping button, your dwarf will repeatedly say "MUSHROOM! MUSHROOM?! MUSHROOM." Once, I spent a silly amount of time doing so, while my allies were doing their own thing. I could certainly say the host was certainly not happy about it, similar whenever I tried to solo objectives. Apparently the guides and rule book (idk what they are talking about honestly) does not allow that.

The community is SUPER helpful too with how swift and efficient they are, thanks to the lack of voting system. There will be no drama or anything, they will type "..." and you will be gone.

Both the discord and Community sounds like a great place you will like, if that's what you are into! The feeling of losing all progress you did from that almost 1 hour game is unmatched, similar to getting kicked on last wave in MvM; I think it does feel like character building though. It really prepares you for some random Deep Dive crashes where you don't get anything, and maybe some day you will be as good as them.

[h2]QOL Pass & Late Game[/h2]
I think Ghost Ship did something right by adding free battle pass that lasts indefinitely instead of more biomes or new content. I'm not sure where they got they idea from, but hey more mil-sim and rainbow gun skins!! I know that whenever someone asks me about it, they would prolly say 'that's hideous, why would they add that?' and then I never really see them ever playing again. I don't know if that is related or was not related. I think I confused myself too.

Why not, besides well umm .. what is Nexus Mods anyway? Never heard of them, really. They kept telling me using sandbox modification in Singleplayer is illegal or something. I think I can trust them, since its their game after all.

Although, I'm not super sure what the community meant by the end-game content is lacking. I do think the weapon system is neat with how you need to wait 20-50 weeks or about 50-100 hours for the overclock you want. There are like hundreds of them, which are just sidegrades from one another. And you know what? 

They are not wrong, But, BUT! - I do like having a reason to have to log back in every week for a chance for 1+3 chance to roll a weekly core for a weapon or class I would never use. That's a respectful REALISM to have; It feels like a job! But LISTEN, when I do finally get what I wanted after another 100 hours, the ADHD in my brain somehow gave me a DOPAMINE to play another 50 hours or so. Something like that, yus. More please!

Not to mention how you can promote your dwarf to get a different colored border. Bronze. Silver. Gold. Platinum? 
[h3]Whoa no way. My brain goes "!!!" every single time.[/h3]
It’s a new color to look at. I like the colors. Its still something new to look at. I mean the gray rocks and music do feel nice, but COLORS. The promotion badges genuinely made me cry. I didn't know I needed that. I don't know why I kept playing. My campus' therapist told me that they should have named my son after GSG, and not Relogic after my Mediumcore Zenith seed experience.

[h2]Remarks[/h2]
I cannot express how else - or heck where I would be without this game!

I'm so happy to be put in this situation while getting my ass hammered on Hazard ~5+ thanks to its very realistic and friendly game design. I sure love getting bombed from literally everywhere thanks to its new corespawn bug design!! It really keeps me aware of my surroundings. Other games should really maybe teach PvE players to not fall asleep more.
P.S sorr my cat told me to stop.

Gold and Scales! uwu

review:
There's not much I can say that the other overwhelmingly positive reviews already didn't cover. The game is probably my favorite of its kind. You pick a procedurally generated mission and launch into a procedurally generated map and do that mission while also mining around for ore and gold to buy some cosmetics and upgrades.

90% of the progression is cosmetic. On top of that, there is NO FOMO when it comes to cosmetics (except for anniversary hats) ALL cosmetics are unlockable and ALL previous season passes are still available for you to progress through. 

The game gets updated almost constantly and there's enough content to last you hundreds of hours. 

Also, I think I ♥♥♥♥♥♥ around in the lobby more than actually playing the game. Why is there a functional prop hunt minigame there?? Amazing.

review:
Deep Rock Galactic is a very charming game. It is one of those perfect experiences you can comfortably return to every once in a while for a run or two, packed with personality and fresh challenges. The game features fun missions and dynamic modifiers and enemies that consistently keep the experience feeling fresh.

To tackle these challenges, you have four distinct classes, each equipped with completely different loadouts and unique ways to conquer this hostile space rock. It is incredibly fun to experiment and tune your chosen class to perfectly fit your preferred playstyle.

The overclock system will undoubtedly be your biggest grind in the endgame. Unfortunately, it is not always easy to get the exact overclock you want, meaning much of your late game progression will be spent chasing after that one specific build enabler. Thankfully, the methods for earning these overclocks are varied and fun, even if the overall process is a bit slow.

Beyond the gameplay mechanics, the community itself is incredibly welcoming and wholesome. Just make sure you follow the etiquette of the caves, because you better not double dip on the resupply pod without asking.

I highly recommend this game if you love the four disposable idiots killing hordes of stuff genre, or if you just want a quirky, high action game for yourself or a friend group to pick up and revisit every now and then.

review:
If you're looking for a review and not a niche critique you should probably keep scrolling, though I'll say the game is satisfyingly punchy, has great character and various zones and weapons to explore. If you like Left 4 Dead and are wanting a little novelty, chances are you'll probably like this game too. I personally like this game, but it falls completely flat in the implementation of its reward structures. 

Typically when selecting a mission to complete, you'll choose a mission you have an assignment for. On successful completion of these assignment missions, you are awarded various currencies and gold to spend on shop goodies. Unfortunately, the implementation of missions and rewards feels at odds with the way the game is intended to be played in a couple prominent ways; multiplayer friction and difficulty vs play incentives.

For multiplayer, the issue is extremely straight forward: each player in a lobby is going to have a different mission for their assignment; meaning you cannot progress multiple players' missions at the same time. This obviously sucks for playing with friends, and will likely result in players rotating whose missions are completed each round. 

The part that baffles me is that the vast majority of missions have absolutely no reason to be asynchronous, given they're just random filler to get you exploring and exterminating Hoxxes. There's no story associated with most missions, so having them be exclusionary feels like a slap in the face to multiplayer incentives. You're going to complete your missions far quicker in solo lobbies; forcing people to choose between either progressing their missions at an even remotely reasonable pace alone, or playing with friends and slowing reward gain drastically.

My other issue with the game relates to the difficulty options for missions, and how that clashes with the rewards handed out on mission completions. In Left 4 Dead, the missions you run and difficulties you choose do not have any greater bearing on the game beyond moment-to-moment play. There are no in-game rewards or currencies to receive; difficulty is encouraged solely through the appeal of mastery.

Unfortunately, a similar implementation of difficulty in Deep Rock Galactic is at odds with the reward structure. Given that assignment rewards are only obtained from successful missions, players are incentivised to play on lower difficulties to breeze through their missions to guarantee their mission rewards. Forcing players to choose a fun and challenging gameplay experience or a boring but highly efficient one sucks.

I hope in the future that some of these systems undergo some renovations. At the very least, you should be able to (for weekly core and resource missions) sync your assignment with other players in the lobby.

review:
Пещеры... Изучать пещерные котокомбы - это интересное занятие. Но когда на тебя с командой лезет местный чудовищный по своим размерам рой жуков, то игра превращается в веселое месиво!
   Лично у меня от этой игры никогда не было негатива, я в ней раслабляюсь. И очень хорошо запоминаются не только эпичные и красочные сражения, но и поражения. Например когда на вашу команду несётся два дредноута, разведчик умудрился разбиться, инженер не успел собрать турель, бурильщик бегает по кругу, отбрасывая взрывчатку, а я стою с миниганом, пытаюсь спасать ситуацию и в итоге умираю. 
   Я с друзьями доволен! Rock and stone!

review_english: Caves... Studying cave cat-combs is an interesting activity. But when a local swarm of beetles, monstrous in size, attacks you and your team, the game turns into a fun mess! Personally, I have never had any negative experiences with this game; I relax in it. And not only epic and colorful battles are remembered very well, but also defeats. For example, when two dreadnoughts are rushing towards your team, the scout managed to crash, the engineer did not have time to assemble the turret, the driller runs in a circle, throwing explosives, and I stand with a minigun, trying to save the situation and end up dying. My friends and I are happy! Rock and stone!

review:
11/10 - A must-play if you're a fan of FPS and Metroid looking for a variety filled challenge.
-------------------------
I've been playing on Xbox since 2022 and logged roughly 2k hours since, but only recently did I choose to start from scratch on Steam for fun. The game not only runs buttery smooth even with a few mods, but DRG just soars with everything it meant to: Amazing action and gameplay, rock-solid frame rate, amazing community, and whole catalogues of mods to try with built-in parts of the menu and startup just for those mods. 
My favorite parts of DRG are the cosmetics and progression. You can customize everything about your dwarves from head to toe, armor, armor paint, and tons of facial hair to name a few. But the best part is no matter what you unlock or how you unlock it, the game always makes you feel good about it like you earned it.
------------------
FOR ROCK AND STONE!!
(And go play Rogue Core if you want something even harder than DRG)

review: Подпивасные приключения 4 дворфов. А если серьезно, то это очень хороший проект который заставляет испытать множество эмоций: за 1 миссию ты можешь посмеяться с вечной WE'RE RICH, быть на панике из за роя, когда у тебя нет патронов, а нитры не хватает, искренне переживать за последнего оставшегося бурильщика, который на последних хп пытается нас реснуть, и это все за несчастные 30-40 минут миссии . Да что уж тут говорить: спустя 2 часа игры я стал заставлять своих друзей купить эту игру чтобы они тоже прочуствовали этот вайб опасной шахты и братских дворфов. Сейчас пишу этот отзыв с 14 часами, но подозреваю совсем скоро сверху будет цифра намного больше. Покупайте, не пожалеете

review_english: Drinking adventures of 4 dwarves. But seriously, this is a very good project that makes you experience a lot of emotions in 1 mission, you can laugh with the eternal WE'RE RICH, be in a panic because of the swarm when you have no cartridges and not enough nitrate, sincerely worry about the last remaining driller, who is trying to kill us with his last HP, and all this in the unfortunate 30-40 minutes of the mission. What can I say, after 2 hours of playing, I began to force my friends to buy this game so that they, too, could feel this vibe of a dangerous mine and brotherly dwarves. Now I’m writing this review with 14 hours, but I suspect that very soon the figure will be much higher. Buy it, you won't regret it

review:
잘 만든 게임이에요
게임하면서 계속 새로운 무기 조합, 오버클록 써보는게 재밌었고 게임에 익숙해져 더 어려운 난이도를 원하면 게임 내에서도, 모드로도 난이도를 조절할 수 있어서 좋아요.
짧으면 5분 길면 40분까지 미션에 따라 게임 플레이 시간도 다양한것도 큰 이점이에요
동료들과 V를 눌러 경례하고 맥주를 쏴주기도 하고 상호작용 할 수 있는게 많아서 친구들이랑 해도, 공개방 모르는 사람들과 해도 즐거워요

다만 후속작 상태+현재 개발진들 태도 때문에 따봉 주고 싶진 않네요
이 게임 업데이트 1년씩 미루고 나온 결과물이 너무 아쉬워요

시즌 6에서 나온 새로운 지형은 쓸데없이 거대하고 복잡한데 새로운 미션은 또 쓸데없이 길어서 안 좋은 쪽으로 시너지가 났어요. 핏죠를 비롯한 적들도 불쾌했고요.
이것보다 안 좋아질 수 있을까 생각하던 찰나 새로운 주의보가 추가 되었는데, 잘 쳐줘야 리소파지 주의보보단 조금 낫다 정도에요. 지형이 좁게 뜨면 안그래도 빠른 적들을 피할 수가 없었고 코어스톤을 공격하는 즉시 이벤트 시작이라 스웜 적들 처리하다 누가 총으로 쏴버려서, 준비되지 않은 팀원들이 다 눕는 경우도 있었어요. 시작방에서 생성되어 총알 다 떨어진 상태로 본 미션을 시작해야하는 경우도 있었구요.
제일 안 좋은 점은 게임이 이 상태로 방치되고 있고, 다음 업데이트를 언제 할지 미지수라는 거예요. 아직 플레이 하는 사람들이 꽤 있는 만큼, 방치 엔딩이 나지 않았으면 좋겠네요.

review:
Es un juego desarrollado con cariño, tiene el tipo de detalles que te encontrarías en un juego indie, pero tiene un problema fundamental de diseño y es que su rejugabilidad es entre baja, y lo compensan con mecánicas que usualmente encontrarías en juegos F2P con un pase de batalla.
Me explico
La rejugabilidad del juego se sustenta en base a 4 variables, los tipos de misiones (que son 10), los modificadores de misión (mutators/anomalies, son varios pero no son game changers), el propio personaje que eliges (hay 4), y el layout de las misiones. ¿Qué quiere decir esto? que una misma misión puede variar su gameplay en base a una amenaza, al personaje con el que uno juega, y a la generación al azar del nivel, poco más que eso. existen otras variables como las hordas, enemigos especiales, y demás, pero realmente no cambian en nada la experiencia del juego.
¿Cómo compensa el juego la baja variabilidad de experiencia de juego? con completar objetivos externos a las misiones, para desbloquear un arma debes seguir este orden de misiones, para desbloquear este cosmético en este evento debes buscar Ossium en las misiones, para desbloquear un nuevo slot de habilidad debes subir el personaje de nivel haciendo estas misiones, para desbloquear... misión.
Creo que se ve el problema, para avanzar el juego te dicta que misiones hacer de forma constante, y estas misiones pueden o no tener variaciones que las hagan distintas, usualmente NO es así y terminas haciendo las mismas misiones de forma constante, esto no seria un problema de no ser porque el juego no te premia por explorar otras misiones de forma solitaria, conseguir los recursos para mejorar un arma pierde inmediatamente su sentido si ya tienes un modificador que te sirve.
El juego es consciente de esto, y añade más capas de eventos, es hasta chistoso, hay un árbol de habilidades que sirve para desbloquear cosméticos en base al season pass, es una idea que parece salida del mismo clash royale, pero sinceramente es divertida. Y si te aburres de todo esto, y llegas al final de la experiencia, te da eventos al azar que cambian la experiencia de la misión, pero se tienen que activar usando llaves, o similares, ESTE ultimo detalle es mi problema más grande, los eventos que tienen el potencial de cambiar la experiencia de juego se desbloquean cerca del final del camino, hay que jugar semanalmente para tener una oportunidad de jugarlos, y aunque no tengas la oportunidad, el juego te los presenta aunque estés en nivel 1 en primera misión. Esta completamente diseñado para ser una experiencia meh, pero que quieras terminarla porque esperas que te de algo más, sinceramente el juego seria mejor con mas variables por misión, o al menos distintos resultados/finales en cada una.
El momento más chistoso que recuerdo del juego fue cuando estábamos llegando a la capsula de escape y me noquearon en la puerta, pero como todos ya estaban adentro, la capsula se fue dejándome ahí a menos de 3 metros, la misión ni siquiera la recuerdo, realmente no tuvo impacto distinto a las otras misiones del juego, lo que marco la experiencia de ese momento fue simplemente la situación distinta a lo usual donde todos dijimos "el juego permitía abandonar a un compañero a tan solo 3 metros?"
Quizás la comparativa sea mala, pero Barotrauma con un diseño similar de cumplir misiones para avanzar, tiene más experiencia de juego y no tuvo que insertar tanta parafernalia externa a las misiones para cumplirlo, simplemente mantenerse fiel a cumplir lo mismo, pero que se sienta distinto.

review_english: It's a lovingly developed game, it has the kind of details that you would find in an indie game, but it has a fundamental design problem and that is that its replayability is low, and they make up for it with mechanics that you would usually find in F2P games with a battle pass. Let me explain. The replayability of the game is based on 4 variables, the types of missions (there are 10), the mission modifiers (mutator anomalies, there are several but they are not game changers), the character you choose (there are 4), and the layout of the missions. What does this mean? that the same mission can vary its gameplay based on a threat, the character one plays with, and the random generation of the level, little more than that. There are other variables such as hordes, special enemies, and so on, but they don't really change the game experience at all. How does the game compensate for low gameplay variability? with completing objectives external to the missions, to unlock a weapon you must follow this order of missions, to unlock this cosmetic in this event you must search for Ossium in the missions, to unlock a new skill slot you must level up the character by doing these missions, to unlock... mission. I think you see the problem, to advance the game it dictates which missions to do constantly, and these missions may or may not have variations that make them different, usually this is NOT the case and you end up doing the same missions constantly, this would not be a problem if it were not for the fact that the game does not reward you for exploring other missions alone, obtaining the resources to improve a weapon immediately loses its meaning if you already have a modifier that works for you. The game is aware of this, and adds more layers of events, it's even funny, there is a skill tree that is used to unlock cosmetics based on the season pass, it's an idea that seems straight out of Clash Royale itself, but it's honestly fun. And if you get bored of all this, and you reach the end of the experience, it gives you random events that change the experience of the mission, but they have to be activated using keys, or similar, THIS last detail is my biggest problem, the events that have the potential to change the gaming experience are unlocked near the end of the path, you have to play weekly to have a chance to play them, and even if you don't have the opportunity, the game presents them to you even if you are at level 1 in the first mission. It is completely designed to be a meh experience, but if you want to finish it because you hope it will give you something more, honestly the game would be better with more variables per mission, or at least different final results in each one. The funniest moment I remember from the game was when we were reaching the escape capsule and they knocked me out at the door, but since everyone was already inside, the capsule left, leaving me there less than 3 meters away. I don't even remember the mission, it really didn't have a different impact than the other missions in the game. What marked the experience of that moment was simply the different situation than usual where we all said "the game allowed you to abandon a companion just 3 meters away?" Perhaps the comparison is bad, but Barotrauma, with a similar design of completing missions to advance, has more gaming experience and did not have to insert so much external paraphernalia into the missions to complete it, simply staying true to completing the same thing, but making it feel different.

review:
I'm still learning what everything does as the game has a lot of things to learn.

The controls are responsive, sound effects are good, weapons handle well, lots of character customisation, different builds for each character, different weapon combo's, good mission variation with various enemies that can attack while mining an area or returning to the mule.

They will also attack when returning to the pod once a mission is complete.

After each successful mission your character earns XP to level up, which will unlock other missions or areas to explore.

It's quite an addictive game.  Get in, mine what you can and get out, hopefully, in one piece. :D

I'm enjoying what I've played so far, but I have a lot more to learn.

El juego es uno de esos que tienen regujabilidad infinita con misiones, pero la forma en lo que lo hace es misiones en una rotacion de mapas limitado con una combinacion limitada de enemigos irritantes para subir de nivel y desbloquear una mejora unica para un arma y que se repita el proceso. El problema aca es que va con mecanicas de juego de gacha por lo que podria no tocarte uno para tu clase. Se vuelve una tarea tipo sisifo como intentar extender un chicle que ya acabo el sabor

Los cosmeticos tambien son un arbol que se avanza con recursos que se vuelven más lentos de conseguir mientras más avanzas y para avanzar de lado en lado tienes que tambien obtener otras cosas para clases que tu no juegas. Es primera vez que veo un arbol para cosmeticos y espero que sea el ultimo. Genuinamente a quien se le ocurrio esta mierda

Lo mejor que tiene el juego la verdad es que tienen un clon del Prop Hunt pero para eso ponte el Garrys Mod y era.

review_english: The game is one of those that has infinite regujability with missions, but the way it does it is missions on a limited map rotation with a limited combination of irritating enemies to level up and unlock a unique upgrade for a weapon and repeat the process. The problem here is that it uses gacha game mechanics so you might not get one for your class. It becomes a Sisyphus type task like trying to extend a piece of gum that has already run out of flavor. Cosmetics are also a tree that is advanced with resources that become slower to obtain the further you advance and to advance from side to side you have to also obtain other things for classes that you do not play. It's the first time I've seen a tree for cosmetics and I hope it's the last. Genuinely, who came up with this shit? The best thing about the game, the truth is that they have a Prop Hunt clone, but for that, use the Garrys Mod and it was.

review: Deep Rock Galactic — это великолепная командная игра, где главное — это не просто стрельба по жукам или добыча ресурсов, а уникальная дворфийская атмосфера. Отдельного восторга заслуживает возможность после напряженной смены в пещерах потанцевать со своей КОМАНДОЙ в баре, выпить пива и поднять кружку в память об ушедших товарищах. В сочетании уютного безумия, кооперации и дворфийского братства кроется гениальность игры. Очень рекомендую всем, у кого есть друзья для совместной игры.

review_english: Deep Rock Galactic is a great team game where the main thing is not just shooting bugs or mining resources, but a unique dwarven atmosphere. The opportunity, after a busy shift in the caves, to dance with your TEAM in the bar, drink beer and raise a mug in memory of departed comrades deserves special delight. The game's genius lies in the combination of cozy madness, cooperation, and dwarven brotherhood. I highly recommend it to anyone who has friends to play with.

review:
Deep Rock Galactic — игра на самом деле отличная. Она забавная, атмосферная, здорово радует своей процедурной генерацией пещер и уникальными классами. Поначалу игра вызывает дикий восторг: копаешь руду, стреляешь по жучкам, пьёшь пиво на станции — всё хорошо.

Но вот в чём главный нюанс: одному здесь делать нечего. Сюжета нет, играешь либо соло, либо со случайными игроками и друзьями, если такие есть. 

С рандомными игроками уже веселее, это правда. Бывают забавные моменты, когда вы вместе теряетесь, сдерживаете орду и кричите «Rock and Stone!». Но, во-первых, это часто очень трудно. Нет координации — и любая миссия превращается в хаос, где ты трижды упал, а никто тебя не поднял.

А во-вторых (и это, пожалуй, самый большой минус), очень трудно играть с ветеранами. Когда ты новичок, который только осваивается в пещерах, и пытаешься не отстать от команды, а к тебе в лобби залетают "дворфы-монстры" с максимальным рангом. Они пролетают всё подземелье за минуту, сметают всё на своём пути, делают миссию за тебя, а ты даже не понял, что произошло. Плетёшься в хвосте, а игра уже закончена. Никакого удовольствия от процесса — просто чувствуешь себя бесполезным мешком с рудой.

А порой тебя просто кикнут, за то что бесполезен.

10 Rock and Stone из 10

review_english: Deep Rock Galactic is a really great game. It's funny, atmospheric, and greatly delights with its procedural generation of caves and unique classes. At first, the game causes wild delight: you dig ore, shoot at bugs, drink beer at the station, everything is fine. But here’s the main nuance: there’s nothing for anyone to do here. There is no plot, you play either solo or with random players and friends, if you have any. It's more fun with random players, that's true. There are fun moments when you get lost together, hold off the horde and shout Rock and Stone! . But first of all, it is often very difficult. There is no coordination and any mission turns into chaos, where you fell three times and no one picked you up. And secondly (and this is perhaps the biggest disadvantage), it is very difficult to play with veterans. When you are a beginner who is just getting used to the caves, and you are trying to keep up with the team, and “dwarf monsters” with the maximum rank fly into your lobby. They fly through the entire dungeon in a minute, sweep away everything in their path, do the mission for you, and you don’t even understand what happened. You are trailing behind, and the game is already over. There is no pleasure in the process, you just feel like a useless sack of ore. And sometimes they will simply kick you for being useless. 10 Rock and Stone out of 10

review:
This is easily one of the best co-op games I’ve played. The whole thing just understands what makes playing with other people fun: everyone has a job, every class feels useful, and even a messy mission can turn into a great story if the team somehow makes it back alive.

The caves are a huge part of the magic. They’re dark, weird, dangerous, and different every time, so even simple objectives feel fresh. Mining, fighting bugs, calling supplies, digging tunnels, saving teammates, rushing back to the drop pod with no ammo left — it all comes together in a way that feels chaotic but never pointless.

I also love how much personality the game has. The dwarves, the voice lines, the beer, the hub area, the upgrades, the “Rock and Stone” energy — it makes the game feel warm and funny without ruining the tension of the missions.

It’s the kind of game that works whether you want a chill run with friends or a complete disaster where everyone is yelling and somehow still laughing. Very easy recommendation. Rock and Stone.

review:
With over 200H on the PS, i can safely say that this game is a banger.
It's such a damn shame that there is no cross progression because starting from 0 does feel a bit unmotivating but i love this game so i can forgive it.
The classes are all pretty balanced and viable.
I do wish that we could maybe see new classes in the future and see some new content that changes the game in a strong and good way.
After 200H the game can start to feel a bit repetitive (obviously) which is why i think a new class could spice things up a bit.
By the way this game has 0 MICROTRANSACTION that are pay to win.
Only cosmetic items, that is a MASSIVE plus from me.
ROCK AND STONE TO THE BONE!

review:
This is probably one of those reviews I ought to have made a while back when I was really into the swing of things with this game rather than waiting until now.

Yes, Deep Rock Galactic is a great game. It's good solo and it's good with friends - you'll have more fun with friends than solo, but if you're like past me and get a weird obsession with playing a game continuously for the better part of several months at a time while you're friends are asleep or at work, you'll definitely get your money's worth out of it solo, it's not impossible. The devs continue to update the game with new content as well, but have also included the option to load past content passes so that you can enjoy previous season experiences freely however you want without having to worry that the game continuously changes too much from when you first bought it (something I'd argue *all* live service games should have built in, because sometimes we just want the perfection of what we got out of the box over the new idea that got slapped on later).

DRG's premise is also amazing. Dwarves in Space is not a new concept by any stretch, but I do legitimately believe that thanks to the success of this game in particular it has made a resurgence in modern scifi (I mean how else can you explain Games Workshop resurrecting a retconned race from early 40k that got axxed because they wanted to separate 40k and Warhammer Fantasy so they could sell more models?). Probably my one regret is that Ghost Ship Games has stated previously that they don't intend to expand the in-game universe of DRG beyond the games that they are currently working on/publishing...which is a legit shame since I'm a huge sucker for traditional D&D-coded fantasy races in space and would love to see more. I'm looking forward to all the future seasons that they have planned and exploring the new biomes, and hopefully one day getting my hands on their other games soon.

Edit - Rock and Stone to the bone!

review:
[h1] 700 Stunden später – und ich grabe immer noch weiter[/h1]

Nach über [b]700 Stunden Spielzeit[/b] kann ich mit voller Überzeugung sagen: [i]Deep Rock Galactic[/i] gehört zu den besten Koop-Spielen, die ich jemals gespielt habe.

[hr][/hr]

[h2] Rock and Stone bis zum Umfallen[/h2]

Was als „nur eine weitere Runde“ beginnt, endet oft Stunden später mit einer epischen Rettungsaktion, einem verzweifelten Kampf gegen eine Glyphiden-Horde oder einer vollkommen chaotischen Flucht zur Rettungskapsel. Und genau das macht dieses Spiel so besonders.

Jede Mission fühlt sich anders an. Die prozedural generierten Höhlen sorgen selbst nach hunderten Stunden noch für Überraschungen. Man kennt die Spielmechaniken in- und auswendig, aber die Situationen, in die das Spiel einen wirft, bleiben frisch und spannend.

[hr][/hr]

[h2] Das beste Koop-Erlebnis seiner Art[/h2]

Die vier Klassen sind perfekt aufeinander abgestimmt. Egal ob Scout, Engineer, Gunner oder Driller – jede Rolle fühlt sich wichtig an und trägt ihren Teil zum Erfolg des Teams bei.

Besonders beeindruckend ist, wie selten man toxischen Mitspielern begegnet. Die Community gehört zu den freundlichsten und hilfsbereitesten, die ich in all den Jahren Gaming erlebt habe.

[b]Rock and Stone![/b] ist nicht einfach nur ein Schlachtruf – es ist eine Lebenseinstellung.

[hr][/hr]

[h2] Entwickler, die ihr Spiel wirklich lieben[/h2]

Ghost Ship Games hat über Jahre hinweg gezeigt, wie man ein Live-Service-Spiel richtig betreibt.

Keine aggressiven Monetarisierungsmodelle.
Keine Battle-Pass-FOMO.
Keine Pay-to-Win-Mechaniken.

Stattdessen gibt es kostenlose Inhalte, neue Missionen, Waffen, Events und Quality-of-Life-Verbesserungen, die das Spiel immer weiter verbessern.

In einer Zeit voller überteuerter DLCs und Mikrotransaktionen ist das fast schon eine Seltenheit.

[hr][/hr]

[h2] Die perfekte Mischung aus Chaos und Können[/h2]

Egal ob entspannte Mineralien-Suche oder Hazard 5+, bei dem das gesamte Team um sein Leben kämpft – Deep Rock Galactic schafft es, sowohl Gelegenheitsspieler als auch Veteranen abzuholen.

Nach 700 Stunden entdecke ich immer noch neue Taktiken, Build-Kombinationen und Momente, die mich zum Lachen bringen.

[hr][/hr]

[h2] Fazit[/h2]

700 Stunden sind normalerweise mehr als genug, um die Schwächen eines Spiels zu erkennen.

Bei Deep Rock Galactic habe ich stattdessen 700 Stunden lang Gründe gefunden, warum ich immer wieder zurückkomme.

[b]Fantastisches Koop-Gameplay.[/b]
[b]Großartige Community.[/b]
[b]Faire Entwickler.[/b]
[b]Unendlich viele unvergessliche Momente.[/b]

Wenn Zwerge, riesige Alien-Spinnen, Miniguns, Explosionen und Teamwork auch nur ansatzweise interessant für euch klingen, dann gibt es eigentlich nur eine Antwort:

[h1] ROCK AND STONE! ⛏️🍺[/h1]

[i]Empfohlen?[/i]

[b]Nach 700 Stunden: Absolut. Ohne jede Einschränkung.[/b]

review:
Pretty good game but does heavily favor group play instead of solo play (grouping with randoms is great too). Some maps are just too big for one player to be searching alone, the extra pair of eyes (and hands) the other players serve as, can drastically cut how long a mission is going to last. 

I don't like how long it takes to unlock your other weapons, it'd be nice if you could get them earlier so you can try them all out sooner and settle on a build you like.

Aside from that, great gun variety, mission variety, class variety. Just a really solid gameplay loop overall that you can easily sink hours into.

review: Deep Rock Galactic has been one of my most played and favorite games for a long time now. Where to even start! All the characters are fun to play, scout being my favorite. Driller being a close second. Grappling hooks are really fun, the flare gun is a very great tool and I adore the Deepcore with AI stability engine. This game has so much content variety and essentially endless build potential and things to do. The easy modding support is amazing especially given some of the extremely nice QOL stuff there is. My only gripe with DRG is the weapon overclock grind, which has been amended a bit by adding them to promotions from season 3 and onwards. But theres still around 150 or so of them and only like a third are transformative and id say fewer are actually class defining. Neuro Toxin Payload, Special Powder, Explosive Chemical Rounds, RJ250, Fat Boy, Cryo Nucleation are some of the most transformative and strong overclocks in the game but thats less than 1/15th of them. A lot tend to just be like +1 magazine worth of ammo or +2 damage which are nice but dont change the game. I do suggest that anyone whos into FPS's and especially co-op horde shooters picks this game up, especially on sale where its only 10 dollars. I think sometimes even down to 7. You really dont have much to lose at that point. So remember to rock and stone or you ain't coming home!

playtime_forever: Deep Rock Galactic is simply That Guy. It is the standard of first person co-op shooters that other developers have to meet. It has great variety of classes, weapons, and at the very least 3 completely different ways to play each of the 24 available weapons. Each weapon has at least 2-3, usually many more ways to build it strong enough for the highest difficulty available. Every class has its separate roles they excel at, and each of the 4 of them is crucial to finishing the mission with the most resources possible and at the fastest time. The game is designed in such a way that players end up coordinating without "stand in a circle" and "ready" checks, the game loop is buttery smooth. The enemy design is very varied, asking the players to take completely different approaches to different threats. Certain elements are better against certain enemies, physical damage is better against others, and the team is required to have both single target and AoE weapons at all times. 11 missions with hundreds of unique "rooms" the caves generate from, as well as random modifiers to the missions that give higher rewards but increase the difficulty with harder enemies, or draining health, or special events that can spawn during every single run, ensure that the game stays fresh for a very long time. This game is a 9 10. The only thing holding this game back from having a perfect rating is that the meta progression could've been faster, and the perks should be reworked to matter more.

review:
Rock and stone. This game is good, better with friends. The maps are procedurally generated and with several regions to choose from, it take a while to feel stale. The gameplay loop is good, especially when you swap between classes. There are multiple jobs to play through and after years of updates, there are tons of side objectives that can spawn or happen. Because of these random side objectives and procedural map generation, you can binge this game for a few hours and not see the same situation. Eventually the end game becomes grinding out "overclocks" for your characters, which are boosts to your weapons to keep the gameplay loop fresh.

review:
Look, I’ve played a lot of shooters, but nothing quite scratches the itch like Deep Rock Galactic. On paper, it sounds simple: you’re a space dwarf, you go underground, you mine rocks, you shoot giant bugs, and you get out. But the execution? Absolute perfection. It is a masterclass in game design, community building, and pure, unadulterated fun.

Here is exactly why DRG deserves every ounce of praise it gets.

1. The Mining Just Feels Right
In most games, resource gathering is a chore. It’s the boring thing you have to do so you can get back to the "real" game. Not here. The literal act of swinging your pickaxe into a vein of Gold or Nitra is incredibly satisfying. Ghost Ship Games nailed the audio design and tactile feedback. When you hit a chunk of Morkite and it makes that distinct, metallic clink sound, your brain instantly shoots out a hit of dopamine.

Add in the fact that the caves are 100% destructible, and the gameplay loop becomes an absolute blast. Need to get to a high ledge? Dig a staircase. Giant swarm coming? Drill a bunker. You have total freedom to reshape the environment to your advantage, making every single mission feel dynamic.

2. Devs Who Actually Care (And Free Seasonal Events!)
In an era where modern gaming is plagued by predatory microtransactions, battle passes that feel like a second job, and corporate greed, Ghost Ship Games stands out like a beacon of hope. They genuinely love their player base, and it shows in how they handle live service.

The seasonal events aren't cash grabs. They are massive, completely free updates packed with new content, weapons, overclocks, and cosmetic trees. Missed a previous season? No worries—none of the content is gone forever. Everything gets put right into the standard loot pool, meaning there is zero FOMO (Fear Of Missing Out). When the holidays roll around, the Space Rig gets decked out with hilarious decorations, and the devs throw in special event bonus challenges that are just pure fun. They set the gold standard for what game developers should be.

3. The Voice Lines are Instant Classics
You cannot talk about DRG without talking about the voice acting. The dwarves are overflowing with personality, and the voice lines are endlessly quotable. Whether you're spamming the laser pointer at a chunk of gold shouting "WE'RE RICH!" or pointing at a glowing mushroom just to hear "MUSHROOM," the game leans into its own absurdity perfectly.

And, of course, there’s the universal binding agent of the entire community: "ROCK AND STONE!" Pressing a single button to salute your teammates does more for team morale than any voice chat ever could. You accidentally fell off a cliff? Rock and Stone. You just blew up a massive Glyphod Dreadnought? Rock and Stone. You're just standing around doing nothing? Rock and Stone. It creates an instant bond with random players that you just don't find in other gaming communities.

4. Clocking Out: From a Rough Drop to a Beer-Fueled Marathon
The absolute best part of this game, though, is the contrast between the stress of the job and the absolute vibe of the Space Rig afterward.

You could have the most stressful, high-intensity Hazard 5 mission where everything goes wrong. You run out of ammo, Bulk Detonators are tearing through the caves, Cave Leeches are grabbing people from the ceiling, and you barely make it into the Drop Pod with one health point remaining. Your hands are shaking, your eyes are watering, and you're mentally exhausted.

But then, the loading screen finishes. You spawn back into the Space Rig.

The stress instantly evaporates. You walk right over to the Abyss Bar, order a round of Pots 'O' Gold or Oily Oafs from Lloyd, slam them down, and someone immediately runs over to hit the Jukebox. Within thirty seconds, a brutal, exhausting day of mining transforms into a chaotic beer-dancing marathon. You're kicking barrels into the launch bay, spinning around on the dance floor, and throwing mugs at each other until someone drinks a Blackout Stout and collapses on the floor.

The Verdict
Deep Rock Galactic isn't just a great game because the mechanics are solid (which they are). It’s a great game because it has a soul. It’s built on teamwork, excellent developer communication, and a community that is genuinely welcoming to greenbeards and greybeards alike.

If you haven't played it yet, Dont even think about questioning it, grab your pickaxe, load your shotgun, and get down there.

Rock and Stone!

review:
I should've written a review for this one a long time ago, but where do I even start? This game is everything i want in a sci fi horde shooter.
[h3] Gameplay [/h3]
+ Gunplay is fantastic. Lots of variety in the different guns available for each class and they're all really fun to shoot. Furthermore, the upgrade system affords a lot of customization towards how you want to use the gun, which gets amplified even further when you consider overclocks, an unlockable upgrade system that can drastically modify how the gun functions.
+ Plenty of progression available. Weapons have plenty of upgrades to unlock, there's lots and lots of cosmetics to get to make your dwarf as heavy duty or as silly looking as you'd like. It took me probably around 200-300 hours just to unlock all the weapon overclocks, and I still haven't gotten all the cosmetic ones.
+ Each mission feels unique. Multiple different biomes with different hazards, each mission is randomly generated (but in the best way), random modifiers on some missions. Missions feel just familiar enough that you're not lost on what to do, but don't feel like you're just doing the same thing over and over.
+ Each of the 4 classes feels like its got its own role and place on the team. Each class has its own set of weapons, its own utility to help the team, its own way of traversing the map. You can have more than one of a class on the same team, but a lot of players prefer to have one of each in a lobby, because they're all useful to have, no class feels underpowered.
+ "Live service" model is the best I've ever seen. All the DLC is strictly cosmetic (though, i might add, even these are worth the money, the cosmetics are great), getting the base game gives you every bit of gameplay content there is to be had. The season passes are free. You can go back and work through old season passes at any time at no cost. 
+ Plenty of options even if you have to play solo. Single player gives you a helper bot that follows your orders and can do basically anything a dwarf can do. Playing in lobbys with randoms is also a great option, everyone is there to have a good time for the most part and there aren't many toxic players (although every once and a while you might run into a bad apple). I've seen some players trek across a map to rez a new player and guide them to extract.
~ There can be a bit of a learning curve at the beginning, since new players need to learn traversal, mission flow, how the classes work together (and what their class is expected to do), not to mention all the enemy types and side content. It can be a bit overwhelming at first, but the player base is generally really beginner friendly and happy to help.
[h3] World [/h3]
+ The theme of "harsh company that only cares about profits sends you to die in a hole" works really well and is very funny at times. 
+ Environments are really nice looking. Dark caves are punctuated with neon lighting. Biomes all look different. Stumbling upon a huge cave with giant pillars of crystal or spouts of lava is still impressive almost 500 hrs in. 
~ The graphics style may be hit or miss for some, but personally i think it looks great. The psuedo low poly style gives the game a lot of charm.

In all honesty if you've gotten this far, DRG is probably for you. I really don't have much negative things to say about it. If you give it a little time to learn the systems and traversal, you'll be rewarded with a highly replayable and unique sci fi shooter.

review:
游戏是好游戏，但是神人太多了，还有各种各样的村规。一边着急高难度无限拔高难度，一边在低难度（5+以上）但是又无限强迫队友达到指定要求。排位机制带来的是炸鱼和糟糕队友的体验。没有段位排位则会带来另一面的乱象，就像泰坦陨落2一样。PVE是村规，PVP则是老登玩家的压迫。
感谢带我入坑和陪我玩的朋友们，以及最后做“拼车”成就，踢桶成就也拿下了3380分，也算没有遗憾了。（逆天鬼船成就系统。）
没有朋友的不建议入手，有朋友一起玩的非常欢迎。
那么就说到这么多了，以后如果没有朋友特意叫的话旅途就走到这里了。
再见了矿工们。

review:
Why didn't I play this game sooner is want I think on the daily during my numerous dives to Infuse Core Matrixes.

To anyone looking for an epic, stylish cooperation shooter where everyone needs to play and public servers are welcoming, this is the game.

Rapid summery of why its awesome :
- The progression of this game is like compressed gold
- The devs are involved and their live-service model cannot possibly earn them money
- The gameplay is made for cooperation.
 

The progression is divided in two main parts. The Promotion and the Chase of Overclocks.
You will start with nothing, though you won't be bombarded with thousands of new weapons you should unlock or things that you can't even touch. All you have is access to the 4 classes with only a weapon and grenades.
The more you continue, the more you unlock the basic gear. More grenades, upgrades to your weapons and at level 10, more weapons. Finally, after much hard work, and better understanding the basics of resources and how to earn equipements, you unlock the key to the rest of the game : a promotion.

With it, you unlock the Forge and Deep-Dives two things that will help in the true game : earning Overclocks.
This is when the game starts to hinder your progression, you can't farm matrix cores. You need to wait, and if the mines continue to call, you can start wanting to upgrades the other classes. This cycle will continue until you have everything the game can offer you which can take a lot of hours. This is a good progression, making you want to earn more while not giving you every tools freely. And the first half of progression is an incredible introduction to the main mechanics of the game and doesn't throw everything at you frame one.



Then there is the live service. Like every game released at this period, this game is a live-service though unlike any other (except one which is MCC which aren't new games). Every season, there is an entirely free 100 reward and cosmetic tree. These haven't any gameplay around it, they are completely optional but the developers made them free just because they respect our time and money we spent.
But there is also the additions to the game. Even if nowadays updates won't change much of the core gameplay of the game, there are still new events and biome added, with enemies and hazards you need to worry about at any missions. The devs continue to update the game and have listened to the community numerous times throughout the 6 years of developpement.
Finally and I am shocked that they did this incredible thing : Every event from previous seasons can still be experienced to this day with the cosmetics still unlockable.
No joke, this is the most consumer friendly live service game on the market. I cannot see a better alternative.



Finally, the 4 classes with a maximum of 4 dwarves per missions (without mods) is obviously screaming for diversity of playstyle within a group.
And the gameplay clearly incentivize this repartition. The classes all have unique traits that makes them needed throughout a mission :
The Driller can drill and their weapons are crowd control machines. Able to reshape the terrain to their advantage, creating alternative routes and displacing objectives. A creative player might also found more uses to take the advantage over the glyphids.
The Scout is your dexterous god, able to go mine every last ores on the cavern ceiling while still able to dish out massive damage on one enemy. Their flare guns makes the caves unable to hide its secrets. Though more of a specialist, they are incredibly useful in a team. Especially paired with
The Engineer. If there is a swiss army knife, it would be them. Multiple angles to cover ? Turrets. A large horde ? Distraction and Semi-auto shotgun. Needing a bridge ? Platforms. A strong bug just spawned ? Grenade Launcher. Though good at seemingly everything, they will need the most ammo of the group and with a big enough swarm, they can be overwelmed.
And if the Glyphid swarm finally arrived and all hope is lost to our 3 dwarves, the Gunner enter in a glorious rescue. They have the firepower and the utility all of the others lack. To destroy swarms, you need the guns, to revive in a crowd of bugs, you need a shield and to transport easily over long distances, you need a zipline. All of these are covered with the Gunner.

Though this perfectly balance class system wouldn't be useful if the enemies weren't good at their job.

I wrote a bit more that I would've liked so I won't larp on it as much. To put it simply, a great deal of bugs are made with teamwork in mind. Stationary creatures which destroyable bullets, weakpoints on the rear of bugs and cave leaches that needs to be killed by a teammate before it releases its prey. The objectives are also all thought with the classes in mind. Some may be easier with the Driller, the Engineer, the Scout or the Gunner. It all depends on the mission and its hazards.



Well I talked enough about this game. Its awesome and anyone that might have the tiniest bit of hesitation should try it. Most people won't regret it.

To finalize, did you know that this game handles mods ? Especially gameplay altering mods which is INSANE. A live service able to entertain the idea of modding. I didn't expect that when I bought it, but I was pleasantly surprised.

review: O Deep Rock Galactic é um jogo na qual se baseia na mineração e batalha contra monstros cavernosos para completr diversos objetivos. No jogo você pode escolher 4 classes diferentes e possui inúmeras habilidades, armas e recursos para aprimorar a sua experiência. No inicio, oque apenas aparenta ser um jogo de excavação se torna em segundo instante um cenário caótico de rochas e pedras. o Deep Rock foi uma experiência impressionante e inédita, apresentando uma temática totalmente inovadora em seu plano. Deep Rock Galactic é um jogo muito bom, e como quase quaisquer outros jogos, fica muito melhor quando experiênciado com amigos ao seu lado. (Valeu ae Nepppp!)

In the game you can choose 4 different classes and have countless skills, weapons and resources to enhance your experience. At first, what only appears to be an excavation game quickly becomes a chaotic scene of rocks and stones. Deep Rock was an impressive and unprecedented experience, presenting a totally innovative theme in its plan. Deep Rock Galactic is a very good game, and like almost any other game, it's much better when experienced with friends by your side. (Thanks Nepppp!)

review:
Plain, stupid fun.

I often dislike live-service games because the gameplay just gets repetitive, and DRG is not immune from that, but it has a character that just keeps me coming back, even after months of not playing. The game is entirely co-op and PvE, meaning your difficulty is almost completely customizable, so I enjoy hopping on and doing some casual missions or pulling some friends together for a deep dive when I'm looking for something more challenging. The gunplay is pleasant and enjoyably customizable. It is a little sad when you unlock all the weapons for a character and can't unlock any more, but matrix cores keep a small element of customizability in the mix pretty much as long as you play. 
It's great with friends, but I've also never had a bad experience playing solo. The community is lively and always seems supportive to me.
It's a space dwarf simulator, both as hardcore and as lightheartedly stupid as you want to make it.

review:
I truly love this game. A friend had gifted it to me so we could play together when Helldivers 2 was in a really bad spot (when isn't it) and it didn't really click with me at first. And then, suddenly it did. I genuinely couldn't stop coming back for more. There's just something so genius about the mix of traditional fantasy with sci-fi that makes for such a rich world full of charm and personality. 
Dwarves mining for resources on an alien planet? What a wonderful idea! The classes all feel good and have a good balance of strengths and weaknesses. All DLC is cosmetic and the passes are always available to you. Soundtrack is great for the atmosphere when exploring and the intensity of swarms and bosses. Customization is great with countless combinations. There's a number of recreational activities on the space rig. BEER AND SEXY DWARVES. What's there not to like? Absolutely strong recommendation. Rock and Stone!

review:
I have attempted to play this game multiple times over. In my previous experiences, my friends would start a high-level mission, not explain anything to me, and I would die posthaste. In general, I do not enjoy multiplayer games because of this kind of phenomena, and like other games in the four-player multiplayer genre, I figured this one would go into my backlog never to be touched again.

However, the launch of *Rogue Core* brought me back, as those friends were occupied playing Rogue Core while hanging out in Discord. When talking to them, I discovered this game has a fully functional singleplayer mode! While the game is a little bit more lonely, I frankly **adore** the singleplayer mode. I have played eight hours of this game in the past 24 hours and plan to play hundreds more, this game has me hooked into it's broad wealth of meaty content.

If for whatever reason you bounced off of this game's multiplayer, but enjoy the process of progression and want a fun and rewarding game that *doesn't* capitalize on FOMO, I think this is a fantastic time sync and am excited to play much, much more. Fantastic game, and you've earned a fan for life.

review:
[h1]The Ultimate Science Fiction Dwarf Fantasy![/h1]

This game is a true marvel, and one I didn't expect. Deep Rock Galactic delicately balances whimsy and seriousness, and it strikes that balance just right: The challenges you face on missions feel deeply real and suck you into the game. That tension is frequently broken up by whimsical mechanics like sliding on pipelines through self-drilled tunnels or just by enjoying a drink, or dance, or both (at the same time) in the lobby. The graphics, sound design and especially the music underline the atmosphere. It grabs on to your inner dwarf. It refines that energy, that feeling, and launches it into deep space! Deep Rock Galactic made me feel the most dwarven I have ever felt playing any video game, ever.

[h1]Mechanics[/h1]
Combat mechanics provide a challenge without being overly punishing for new or casual players. The monsters each have different strengths, weaknesses and attack patterns. They are interesting to learn and introduced gradually so that your skill can keep up with the difficulty. The four classes complement each other well, even when you have only two players in a session. 

[h1]Price[/h1]
I got the game for 8,99€ in a sale, and it's a steal at that price. The non-discounted price of 29,99€ is a bit steep as with most games, but not unreasonably expensive. If you have friends to play with it could be worth it. I think I would personally be happy to pay 20€-25€ based on my current experience with the game.
