review:
Honestly, just buy it.
If you’re tired of toxic competitive shooters and games that feel like a second job, Deep Rock Galactic is exactly the palate cleanser you need. It’s basically Left 4 Dead meets Minecraft, but with space Dwarves, and it absolutely rules.

I've put a ton of hours into this, and it’s easily one of the best co-op experiences out there. Here is the actual breakdown of why it's so good:

The Gameplay Loop
You and up to three others drop into 100% destructible alien caves to mine minerals and fight off massive hordes of bugs. There are four classes—Gunner, Scout, Engineer, and Driller—and none of them feel like an afterthought. The synergy is perfect. An Engineer can shoot a platform onto a wall, the Scout grapples up to mine the gold, and the Driller burns a tunnel straight to the extraction pod when things go south. Every class feels powerful and necessary.

The Best Community in Gaming
Online gaming can be a dumpster fire, but the DRG community is shockingly wholesome. A huge part of this is the dedicated salute button. You hit 'V', your Dwarf screams "Rock and Stone!", and instantly everyone is on the same page. You can queue up with total strangers without mics and still have a flawless, coordinated run because everyone just wants to be a good teammate.

Zero FOMO ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥
The developers (Ghost Ship Games) actually respect their players.

The battle passes are completely free.

If you miss a season, the cosmetics don't disappear forever—they just get moved into the regular in-game loot pools for you to find later.

There’s no aggressive monetization pushing you to spend extra cash just to look cool.

Bottom Line
Whether you're sweating through a high-stakes Hazard 5 dive or just screwing around kicking barrels into the launch bay on the Space Rig, this game is pure fun. If you have a group of friends to play with—or even if you don't—it's worth every penny.

Rock and Stone!

Sometimes, I'll even spend hours just goofing around in the hub for no other reason than because it's fun.

Now, if you'll excuse me, I have to go drink copious amounts of beer during work hours while handling dangerous weapons.  Rock and stone, everyone.

review:
ドワーフになって迫りくるエイリアンと戦いながら洞窟を探検しミッションを達成して帰る。
クラフト要素はないもののマインクラフトの洞窟探索のような感覚で探検ができる。あれが好きな人はきっと楽しめるだろう。いわゆるサンドボックス系なのでピッケルで壁や床などあらゆるところが自由に掘れる。

例としてマインクラフトのイメージを挙げているが戦闘は結構激しくて楽しい。
４つの役職がありそれぞれに固有の武器やガジェットがありプレイスタイルが異なる。
ガトリングで敵殲滅するやつとか、ドリルで道を強引に作るやつとか、フックで高いところにひとっ飛びするやつとかバリエーションがあって楽しい。

PS：ゲームには関係ないけどドワーフの見た目のカスタマイズパーツが多くてそれも良い。
口ひげとあごひげと頬ひげが別の部位として付け替えられるってどうなってんだよ。流石ドワーフ。

review_english: You play as a dwarf and fight the approaching aliens as you explore caves, complete missions, and return home.Although there are no crafting elements, you can explore caves in a similar way to cave exploration in Minecraft.If you like that kind of thing, you'll definitely enjoy it.It's a so-called sandbox style, so you can use an ice ax to dig around walls, floors, and anything else.I'm using the image of Minecraft as an example, but the battles are quite intense and fun.There are 4 roles, and each has its own unique weapons and gadgets, so they have different play styles. It's fun to have variations such as annihilating enemies with Gatling, forcibly creating a path with a drill, and jumping to high places with a hook.PS It's not related to the game, but there are many customization parts for the dwarf's appearance, which is good.What's going on with the fact that the mustache, beard, and whiskers can be replaced as different parts?As expected of a dwarf.

review:
因为玩深岩银河被公司破格录取
面试那天，会议室里坐满了人。
这是我人生最重要的一场面试——某跨国顶尖药企的研发岗。
听说录取率不到0.5%。
所有人西装笔挺，履历一个比一个夸张。
有人手握SCI一区论文，有人在大厂研发部实习过，还有人张口闭口就是「靶点筛选」和「临床前研究」。
我默默坐在角落，心里只有一个念头：
——完了，我只是个打《深岩银河》的。
面试官走了进来。
一个戴着金丝眼镜、气场极强的中年女人，眼神锐利得仿佛能看穿人心。
她翻了翻资料，淡淡开口：
「最后一个问题。」
她抬头，目光扫过所有人。
语气平静得可怕：
「——这里，有谁打《深岩银河》？」
空气，瞬间凝固。
下一秒，全场炸了。
坐我旁边的精英女当场皱眉：
「那不是个又肝又吵的游戏吗？天天挖矿挖矿，听着就累。」
前排的男生露出嫌弃表情：
「我听说那游戏玩家每天都在喊‘Rock and Stone’，跟邪教似的，心理素质得有多差？」
另一个人直接笑出声：
「我们做药研的，讲究精准和效率，谁会去玩那种无脑挖矿的游戏？」
有人甚至低声补刀：
「会玩那个的，大概团队协作是有啦，但科研思维肯定不行。」
我低着头，手心开始冒汗。
——要不要承认？
——讲了是不是直接出局？
⸻
就在这时。
面试官再次开口，语气更冷了几分：
「没有人吗？」
全场沉默。
没有一个人举手。
所有人都在等——等别人踩雷。
我喉咙发紧。
脑海里闪过无数画面：
钻机手挖穿岩层、工程师搭炮台、枪手点灯、侦察兵钩索……
我咬了咬牙。
——算了。
我缓缓举起手。
「我……有在玩。」
⸻
下一秒。
整个会议室，所有人齐刷刷看向我。
那种眼神，就像在看一个主动放弃录取机会的傻子。
刚刚那个精英女甚至轻笑一声：
「勇气可嘉。」
⸻
我心里一沉。
——完了。
然而，
面试官的眼神，突然变了。
她猛地站起身。
「你说什么？」
⸻
我愣住：「我……有在玩《深岩银河》。」
⸻
下一秒——
「啪！」
她直接把手上的履历拍在桌上，声音震得全场发颤。
然后，她走到我面前。
在所有人震惊的目光中——
深深一鞠躬。
⸻
「恭喜你。」
「你被录取了。」
⸻
全场 当场炸裂。
⸻
「什么？！」
「就因为他打游戏？！」
「这什么标准？！」
⸻
精英女直接站起来：
「我有两篇顶刊论文，你选他？！」
⸻
面试官冷冷扫了他们一眼，缓缓开口：
「你们，真的以为这只是游戏？」
⸻
她伸出手指，一字一句：
「第一——团队协作。」
「四人小队，各司其职，缺一不可。」
「这就是研发项目最需要的配合能力。」
⸻
「第二——资源管理。」
「硝石、黄金、稀有矿物，精准采集，高效利用。」
「这就是药物研发中的成本控制与资源优化。」
⸻

⸻

⸻
全场鸦雀无声。

语气前所未有的郑重：

⸻
面试官眼神一亮。
下一秒——

⸻

review_english: Because I played Shenyan Galaxy, I was admitted by the company under special circumstances. On the day of the interview, the conference room was full of people. This was the most important interview in my life. I was working for a research and development position in a top multinational pharmaceutical company. I heard that the acceptance rate was less than 0.5. Everyone was in suits and their resumes were exaggerated. Some had SCI area one papers, some had interned in the R&D department of a large company, and some talked about target screening and preclinical research. I sat silently in the corner with only one thought in my mind. It was over. I was just a person playing Shenyan Galaxy. The interviewer came in. A middle-aged woman with a strong aura wearing gold-rimmed glasses. Her eyes were so sharp that she seemed to be able to see through people's hearts. She flipped through the information and asked the last question calmly. She raised her head and glanced at everyone. Her tone was terrifyingly calm. Who is playing here? Deep Rock Galaxy. The air solidified in an instant. The next second, the whole place exploded. The elite woman sitting next to me frowned on the spot. Isn't that a very lively and noisy game? Isn't it a boring and noisy game? I'm tired after listening to it. The boys in the front row showed disgust. I heard that gamers shout Rock and Stone every day, it’s like a cult, how bad their psychological quality is. Another person just laughed out loud. We do pharmaceutical research, and we pay attention to accuracy and efficiency. Who would play that kind of mindless mining game? The whole place was silent. No one raised their hands. Everyone was waiting for others to step on the thunder. My throat was tight. Countless images flashed through my mind. The driller dug through the rock formation. The engineer built the fort. The interviewer's eyes suddenly changed. She stood up suddenly. What did you say? I was stunned. I was playing Deep Rock Galaxy. The next second, bang, she slapped her resume on the table. Her voice shook the whole audience. Then she walked up to me. In everyone's shocked eyes, she bowed deeply. Congratulations. You were admitted. The whole audience exploded on the spot. What? Just because he plays games. What kind of standards is this? Elite women stood up directly. I have two top-ranked papers. You choose him. The interviewer glanced at them coldly. Slowly speaking, you guys really thought this was just a game. She stretched out her finger and said one word at a time. First, teamwork. A team of four, each performing his own duties. Each one is indispensable. This is the coordination ability that is most needed for research and development projects. Second, resource management. Saltpeter, gold, rare minerals, accurate collection and efficient utilization. This is cost control and resource optimization in drug research and development. Third, crisis management. Insect tide incoming. Terrain collapse. Teammate falls to the ground. Instant judgment. Quick response. Turnaround. This is the ability to cope with emergencies in the laboratory. She paused, her eyes sharp. And you guys can't even understand a multiplayer cooperative game. What's the point of talking about team development? The whole room was silent. The interviewer turned to look at me with an unprecedented solemn tone. Tell me what is the sentence you say most often. I was stunned for a second, and blurted out Rock and Stone almost instinctively. The interviewer's eyes lit up. The next second she whispered "To the bone". At that moment, I knew I won. A few minutes later, outside the conference room, those who just laughed at me all gathered around. Brother, how should newbies play in Deep Rock Galaxy? Are you a main drill player or an engineer? Give me a try. I just downloaded it. See if I can play with this configuration. I smiled softly and looked up into the distance. My tone was light, but with an irrepressible edge. Now who dares to say that there is no future in Deep Rock Galaxy?

review:
Recommended.

Deadzone: Rogue does exactly what it promises. It puts you in a space station full of hostile machines, hands you a growing arsenal of ridiculous weapons, and dares you to survive. The roguelite loop is tight, the build variety is genuinely impressive, and the gunplay has real punch behind it.

The element system is where it gets interesting. Turning a basic shotgun into something that spreads void damage across a crowd, or icing an entire corridor of enemies with a modified SMG, gives every run a different feel. You're not just upgrading, you're building a playstyle from scratch each time.

Co-op makes it even better. Coordinating builds with a friend and watching your combined chaos tear through a boss feels great.

For fans of roguelite shooters this is an easy pick up.

review:
Deep Rock Galactic might be the best example of developers understanding exactly what makes co-op games fun instead of what makes them addictive.

On paper, “space dwarves mining bugs underground” sounds like a joke premise somebody came up with at 2am. In practice, it’s one of the tightest co-op experiences ever made.

Everything in this game works because the design philosophy is incredibly disciplined. Every class matters. The Scout feels different from the Engineer, who feels different from the Driller, who feels different from the Gunner, and all of them fit together naturally without forcing rigid MMO-style roles. A good team starts feeling like an actual crew instead of four people independently farming XP in the same cave.

The procedural caves are genius because they create constant low-level tension. Sometimes the mission is smooth and professional. Sometimes your extraction pod lands on the ceiling of hell while half the team is out of ammo, on fire, and screaming about detonators. Those disaster moments are where the game becomes unforgettable.

What really surprised me though is how much personality the game has. It understands tone perfectly. The dwarves are ridiculous, but the world still feels grounded enough that you buy into it. The corporate satire, the voice lines, the bar, the absolute obsession with beer and minerals, all of it somehow creates one of the most charming multiplayer communities in gaming.

And unlike a lot of live-service games, Deep Rock Galactic doesn’t feel openly hostile to your free time. You can tell the developers actually want players to enjoy themselves rather than psychologically cornering them into engagement loops every waking moment. That alone makes it feel refreshing.

Also, there are very few games that better capture the feeling of camaraderie under pressure. Holding a tunnel against a swarm while flares burn out and everyone is barely hanging on feels incredible every single time. The game constantly generates those “that was insane” moments organically.

It’s chaotic, cooperative, weirdly wholesome, and absurdly replayable.

Rock and stone forever.

review:
I’m writing this review in the wake of the Rogue Core release. After nearly 500 hours of playtime, I must say this is one of the best games I have ever played — a must-have and a must-try experience for anyone.

I discovered the DRG series through Deep Rock Galactic: Survivor. Then, with the help of YouTube, I remembered there was another older entry in the series. I checked it out, saw the amazing price at the time — only $10 — and thought, “Yeah, why not?” Those $10 were probably one of the best investments I’ve ever made into a video game. I got so much fun out of it, it’s honestly insane.

The concept of the game is simple: you are a dwarf working for a greedy corporation, using questionable equipment that constantly needs fixing, while barely getting paid. But hey, you do it for the love of the job… and maybe to make Mission Control’s life a little easier. Unless, of course, you decide to stuff the drop pod with barrels — then Mission Control might hate you just a little bit :) The combination of mining and killing bugs works surprisingly well together.

I love the art style, and I love the fact that the game can be both challenging and genuinely fun if you put enough time and dedication into it. The procedurally generated missions help the game stay fresh even after your hundredth mission.

I’m mostly a solo player, and I really appreciate that the developers give you the freedom to play however you want. The keyword here is “choice.” If you want to challenge yourself, you can go solo, but to truly experience the game, you can also play with others.

My experience with the DRG community has been nothing but amazing. I can honestly say this is one of — if not *the* most — positive gaming communities I have ever seen. And I’ve played World of Warcraft and League of Legends in the past, so I definitely have experience with online communities…

Here, your “colleagues” don’t judge you — they help you. They get the job done. One time, I was the last survivor of my squad and sadly got blown up by some exploders. Instead of leaving me there and finishing the mission, my team of completely random players came back, revived me, and helped me reach the drop pod safely. After the mission, I thanked them, and all they said was:

“Rock and Stone, Brother. No dwarf left behind.”

That’s something not many games manage to achieve — such a helpful and positive community. I only hope Rogue Core manages to do the same.

Will you rage in this game? Yes. Will you sometimes miss the drop pod? Also yes. But at the end of the day, that’s part of the fun. And if you are a solo player like me, you’ll probably realize it was more of a skill issue than anything else.

I encourage everyone to check out this game and support Ghost Ship Games in general. They have proven to me that they are a genuinely pro-consumer studio. They don’t charge for season passes, and the only things you can buy are cosmetic skins. The fact that they made previous seasons accessible to new players showed me they are not just trying to make a quick buck — they want players to experience everything the game has to offer. If you put time into the game, you can unlock everything.

My only gripe with the game is that the late-game content is not as fleshed out as it could be.

Go buy it, check it out, and I hope to see you guys in a cave somewhere.

ROCK AND STONE!!!

review:
First of all and most important thing.

I hate, HATE and DESPISE the DLC concept

I have EVERY DLCs this game has thrown at me and i only use one or two armors
Why? 
Because i bought the game at a discount and i feel like i cheated the developers out of my money and i vote with my wallet and they deserve it.

The game is simple, fun, usually called "A charismatic L4D2", and i always come back to it, also i made friends that i still talk with all around the globe here.

The characters are charismatic and lovable, even if i still thing Bosco hates me for some reason.

There is no FOMO here, 
You couldnt play Season 1? no worries, just switch seasons on the ingame computer and done, 
You missed some event? No worries, almost everything gets added to the main loot pool.

The comunity is great, and 95% will treat greenbeards (Newcomers) with love, that 5% left?... well... every community has their problematic members... also drillers...

Greenbeards, HEAR ME BEFORE MANAGEMENT GETS TO ME, DONT GET CLOSE TO DRILLERS IF YOU ARE GOING SCOUT, THEY ARE PREDATORS, YOU ARE PREY, GRAPPLE TO HIGH GROUND.


Jokes aside (Management is watching), game is great, comunity is great, developement team is great, the game is less than a "f word" fast food menu, go for it miner, we'll see each other in the mines!

... ALSO DONT LET ANYONE GIVE YOU LEAFLOVERS, ITS POIS

review:
В принципе кайфовый лут-шутер, где вы посещаете множество планет для добычи полезных ресурсов, и уничтожения орд жуков, жаждущих укусить мягонькую жопку дворфа. 
В игре присутствует множество миссий которые обновляются каждые сутки, а также большой арсенал оружия за каждый класс, (к слову их всего 4- разведчик, бурильщик, ганнер и инженер). 
Также есть огромное количество билдов за каждый класс, ну и само собой большое количество пушек на разный вкус, начиная от простого пистолета и заканчивая самонаводящейся ракетницей (точнее этими ракетами можно управлять, но также присутствует автомат с этой же функцией).
В общем игрушка интересная, вам стоит попробовать (тем более её частенько продают со скидкой). Уверен на десяток часов точно затянет.

review_english: Basically a cool loot shooter where you visit many planets to extract useful resources and destroy hordes of bugs eager to bite the dwarf's soft ass. The game has many missions that are updated every day, as well as a large arsenal of weapons for each class (by the way, there are only 4 of them - scout, driller, gunner and engineer). There are also a huge number of builds for each class, and of course a large number of guns for different tastes, from a simple pistol to a homing rocket launcher (more precisely, these rockets can be controlled, but there is also a machine gun with the same function). In general, the toy is interesting, you should try it (especially since it is often sold at a discount). I'm sure it will definitely drag on for ten hours.

review:
Un jeu vraiment fun, à jouer en coop jusqu'à 4. Peut aussi ce jouer en solo. La variété de missions, de mécaniques, et de façon de s'équiper, ainsi que la génération aléatoire des maps donnent au jeu une rejouabilité quasi infini. Il y a aussi toutes les difficultés, de bien débutant à bien épique. 
Des nains qui minent, et qui dégomment des insectes de l'espace. Mélange de chaos, d'adrénaline, de gestion des ressources, de stratégie et de travail d'équipe.
La communauté est chouette, les cosmétiques sont fun, et il y a régulièrement des évènements et des "saisons" le tout sans frais supplémentaires ! (Les seuls DLC sont purement cosmétique).
Les easters eggs et le contenu extra sont marrant.
Difficile de croire que tout ça tienne en si peu de place, et pour un prix plus qu'abordable !
Rock and Stone !

review_english: A really fun game, to play in coop for up to 4. Can also be played solo. The variety of missions, mechanics, and ways of equipping yourself, as well as the random generation of maps give the game almost infinite replayability. There are also all difficulties, from very beginner to very epic. Dwarfs who mine, and who destroy space insects. Mixture of chaos, adrenaline, resource management, strategy and teamwork. The community is great, the cosmetics are fun, and there are regular events and “seasons” all at no additional cost! (The only DLCs are purely cosmetic). Easter eggs and extra content are fun. It's hard to believe that all of this fits in so little space, and for a more than affordable price! Rock and Stone!

review:
I can honestly say that Deep Rock Galactic is the best co-op shooter out there. Let me explain. 



To start off, this game has the best community out of any game I have ever played. They are always willing to help you out, make dumb jokes, and laugh at the many running gags and inside jokes within the game. 

Actual gameplay wise, the combat is smooth and incredibly satisfying. There are lots of customization options with your weapons and four different classes to play, all of which are VERY different and support certain playstyles. For example, Driller specializes in crowd control and making tunnels, and Gunner specializes in single-target damage and protecting teammates. The combat of this game consists mostly of tackling huge swarms, no, ARMIES of giant horrifying bugs. Killing them with the many weapons in the game always feels satisfying and awesome, no matter how many missions you've played. 

The developers care deeply about their community, and they show it in-game. There are countless built-in running gags and inside jokes, they give ACTUALLY USEFUL TIPS to you, and even input player feedback. This is rare nowadays, as many popular games are just run by corporate slop, but not this one. 

This game does randomly-generated terrain RIGHT. EVERY SINGLE cave is different, and no cave will EVER be the same. The caves are scattered with loot, monsters, and quirks that other caves won't have, at least not in the same way. 

Speaking of which, this game is unrepetitive. Every single mission feels fresh and new, No matter how many times I've played it, it's ALWAYS fun. 

Teamwork is strongly encouraged by the game. It encourages sticking together, building bonds with your teammates, and leaving no one behind. This builds a great community, and makes you feel proud and helpful towards your teammates. This game is amazing with friends! 

The game does SUCH a good job about encouraging teamwork and making you feel satisfied that you feel truly immersed. I have never felt more patriotic to a nation that doesn't exist. 

The only legitimate complaints I have about this game are that 1, Industrial Sabotage exists and 2, the healing system is total and complete ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥. The hell you mean I can only heal through resupply pods and crystals that only appear once or twice per room?! 

Also, there's a bar where you can drink alcohol with your buddies and get super drunk. You can even go on missions while drunk, which is absolutely hilarious. 

10/10, would absolutely recommend.

review:
I am a rock and stone enthusiast who approves of this game.
This game reminds me a lot of Killing Floor, but with a short objective(s) (usually assembling, mining and collecting things, oil piping), and a fully destructible environment vis-a-vis Minecraft-esque caves.

Gameplay progression and loop are very fun, charming art style, very good music, and the writing is pretty funny, if not growing repetitive fairly quickly. Droppîng into active matches via a server browser might or might not excite you, but it's consistent and easy. 

Only complaint with it is ending a match with randoms, to have to sit through a few screens including loading, to be dropped back at home base, then the random quits and you have to sit through another load to be delivered to a home base in your own server instead of just taking over the server you're already in, or just prompting players to actively choose where they want to go after a match before spitting you out somewhere.

The voice acting is mid--some of the deliveries are good and some feel very unnatural--in the immortal words of Archer "Will you pick an accent and stick with it?". 

highly recommend, appeals to casual players and those who want a challenge

review: I recently transferred my save data from the Xbox version, and I can safely say without a doubt Deep Rock is an incredible experience you should play. In total across both versions I have ~158 hours. There is so much content and the game is incredibly replayable as there's always new ways you can experience it. The later game progression is also really fun too I love collecting the overclocks. The community overall is very welcoming as well. I would rate Deep Rock a solid 10/10 as a horde shooter. Rock and Stone like there's no tomorrow!

Игра обновляется редко, но это не такой существенный минус, т.к. вполне легко себя самого занять в такой многофункциональной игре. Миссий здесь огромное кол-во и с каждым сезоном добавляют новые. Конечно забыл упомянуть, что главное в игре это пить пиво и месить жукови в любой удачный момент кричать ROCK AND STONE. Если всё-таки наскучит игра, не забывайте, что есть моды, которые помимо косметических плюшек могут ещё добавлять новые элементы геймлея. Один из таких модов - MCR. ROCK AND STONE, DWARFS!

review:
Genuinely the perfect Co-op shooter. Maybe a few rough patches here and there, but the simple and fun gameplay never feels too repetitive with a good difficulty curve for those looking for it. Subtle reinforcements to the concepts of teamwork and camaraderie that are fun to engage with (e.g. pressing V to Rock n' Stone) while also having an extremely healthy take on the battle pass/season pass and seasonal events with basically zero FOMO make it such a lovely and stress-free time whenever booting up with friends every so often. It also runs smoothly on almost any machine and has such a small download size that the phrase "But I don't have it downloaded" is legitimately never uttered as an excuse. 

Updates are perhaps a bit infrequent, but that's ok since almost every update has felt so purposeful and rich with things to do, see, and shoot, you just need a little patience. This all comes from someone who legitimately has everything; I've been getting the "Empty" messages for cargo crates and lost equipment for quite some time now, but I still love booting up whenever anyone asks to play.

The only thing I want to even gripe about is not being able to play as a girl Dwarf, but that's more of just a joke-y "What would you fix about the game" kind of criticism that shouldn't be taken TOO seriously...but perhaps, maybe, if it's not too much trouble GSG, please?

review:
Deep Rock Galactic é um jogo bastante divertido, principalmente quando se joga com amigos, aí que a diversão é garantida e o jogo brilha! Os gráficos são ótimos, gameplay bem satisfatória e uma vasta variedade de missões, biomas e modos.

Porém existem duas coisas que eu gostaria de falar sobre:

A primeira e a que mais me incomodou é a iluminação, o sistema de sinalizadores é horrível, e como 100% do jogo se passa em cavernas e você está se movimentando o tempo inteiro, poderiam ter investido em uma mecânica de iluminação melhor! "Ah, mas tem uma classe chamada Batedor que tem sinalizadores potentes que duram mais tempo.", de fato tem, mas você depender de uma classe para poder enxergar é sacanagem! Então sim, a iluminação é um problema enorme.

O segundo ponto é sobre a questão da munição, um jogo como Deep Rock Galactic NÃO deveria ter munição LIMITADA, é um jogo bastante frenético e você ficar administrando munição das suas armas é bem chato e desanimador, pois isso tira bastante do fator diversão, de você ser um anão e estar ali enfrentando hordas e hordas de aliens sem se preocupar com nada além da sua sobrevivência, poderiam fazer como outros jogos do gênero e deixar a munição ilimitada ou pelo menos da arma secundária. É bastante sacanagem você ter que ficar administrando munição da primária e TAMBÉM da secundária e ser obrigado a minerar um minério em específico para chamar um reabastecedor de munições!

Tirando esses dois pontos que falei acima, o jogo é simplesmente incrível, chame seus amigos e terá diversão garantida, minha nota é 8/10, justamente e unicamente devido a questão da iluminação e munições.

O segundo ponto é sobre a questão da munição, um jogo como Deep Rock Galactic NÃO deveria ter munição LIMITADA, é um jogo bastante frenético e você ficar administrando munição das suas armas é bem chato e desanimador, pois isso tira bastante do fator diversão, de você ser um anão e estar ali enfrentando hordas e hordas de aliens sem se preocupar com nada além da sua sobrevivência, poderiam fazer como outros jogos do gênero e deixar a munição ilimitada ou pelo menos da arma secundária. É bastante sacanagem você ter que ficar administrando munição da primária e TAMBÉM da secundária e ser obrigado a minerar um minério em específico para chamar um reabastecedor de munições! Tirando esses dois pontos que falei acima, o jogo é simplesmente incrível, chame seus amigos e terá diversão garantida, minha nota é 8 10, justamente e unicamente devido a questão da iluminação e munições.
review_english: Deep Rock Galactic is a very fun game, especially when playing with friends, where fun is guaranteed and the game shines! The graphics are great, the gameplay is very satisfying and there is a wide variety of missions, biomes and modes. However, there are two things I would like to talk about. The first and the one that bothered me the most is the lighting, the flare system is horrible, and as 100 of the game takes place in caves and you are moving all the time, they could have invested in better lighting mechanics! "Ah, but there is a class called Scout that has powerful flares that last longer.", in fact there is, but depending on a class to be able to see is bullshit! So yes, lighting is a huge problem. The second point is about the issue of ammunition, a game like Deep Rock Galactic should NOT have LIMITED ammunition, it is a very frantic game and having to manage ammunition for your weapons is quite boring and discouraging, as this takes away a lot of the fun factor, of being a dwarf and being there facing hordes and hordes of aliens without worrying about anything other than your survival, they could do like other games of the genre and leave unlimited ammunition or at least the secondary weapon. It's quite annoying that you have to manage ammunition from the primary and ALSO from the secondary and be forced to mine a specific ore to call an ammunition replenisher! Apart from these two points I mentioned above, the game is simply incredible, invite your friends and you'll have guaranteed fun, my rating is 8 out of 10, precisely and solely due to the lighting and ammunition issues.

review:
Viniendo de Helldivers 2 y siendo profundamente decepcionado, solo puedo decir que este juego es increíble. Obviamente no tiene una gran narrativa, así que es importante jugar este juego por lo que ofrece: gameplay, progreso, y pasarlo bien con los amigos. 

Sí que me gustaría que hubiera cosas narrativas más interesantes, pero la variedad de gameplay que ofrece el juego es suficiente para que esté siempre encantado, así que en general disfruto el juego. Además, incluso si no lo juegas muy seguido siempre se refrescan las misiones semanales y los Deep Dives, así que no sientas que has "perdido" la oportunidad de más recursos, y en cambio juega el juego como quieras.

Además, incluso si no lo juegas muy seguido siempre se refrescan las misiones semanales y los Deep Dives, así que no sientas que has "perdido" la oportunidad de más recursos, y en cambio juega el juego como quieras.

Obviously it doesn't have a great narrative, so it's important to play this game for what it offers gameplay, progress, and having fun with friends. Yes, I would like there to be more interesting narrative things, but the variety of gameplay that the game offers is enough to always keep me enchanted, so overall I enjoy the game. Additionally, even if you don't play it very often the weekly missions and Deep Dives are always refreshed, so don't feel like you've "missed" your chance for more resources, and instead play the game however you want.

review: Rock and Stone, товарищи! Наконец дошли руки до покупки этой игры после часов 120 на пиратке. К покупке крайне рекомендую, у игры очень активное сообщество, а вместе веселее и по жукам стрелять, и пиво пить. Игра постоянно генерит веселые и опасные ситуации, которые потом можно повспоминать.  неплохой и оптимизированный графоний на UE5, мясо и кровища(пардон, гемолимфа) очень красиво разлетается. Механика стрельбы приятная, в общем все на высоте. Регулярно выходят новые сезоны, что не дает игре затухнуть. Если у вас есть товарищи, которые могут с вами поиграть, то заставьте их купить тоже. в общем, мой вердикт - 8.5/10. балл снял за отсутствие других озвучек(хотя есть и фанатские) и еще полбалла за слишком высокие цены на пиво. Вперед, товарищи, чтобы Руководство гордилось вами!

I highly recommend purchasing it, the game has a very active community, and together it’s more fun to shoot bugs and drink beer. The game constantly generates funny and dangerous situations that you can later remember. good and optimized graphonium for UE5, meat and blood (sorry, hemolymph) scatters very beautifully. The shooting mechanics are pleasant, in general everything is on point. New seasons are released regularly, which does not allow the game to fade. If you have friends who can play with you, then make them buy it too. in general, my verdict is 8.5 10. Go ahead, comrades, so that the Management is proud of you!

review: This is the perfect template for any Co-Op game in the making. Every mechanic is intuitive, with in-game dialogue automatically explaining anything unknown. Co-operation is a natural series of actions that are nigh invisibly encouraged with every mechanic. The game could use some performance tweaks, but the game is already good enough to get 30~ FPS on a plugged in laptop and it has in-built mod support for that.

review: DRG is just a well rounded, considered game. There are two things I appreciate most that other similar titles in the co-op space miss. First - the balance for solo play is great, and scaling from there to 4 is also great, you don't always need a 4 stack to enjoy the game, and you can enjoy the game anywhere on the spectrum from 1-4 players. Second, all classes and combinations are viable within reason, want to run 4 Engineers just because? absolutely fine. It's nice to be able to play what I want to play at any given time without dragging a team down or being forced into a meta.

review:
Amazing game! 

Danger, Darkness and Dwarves!

If you're someone who likes action and teamwork, I would highly recommend Deep Rock Galactic.
Previous seasons have added so much variety to the cave generation, not to mention the mission types.
The classes are just complex enough to make sense when you're starting out and still provide lots of ways to strategize and make builds with.

The community is great too, public games generally have nice people who will fight fiercely alongside you to finish the mission. (Although I don't recommend jumping onto higher difficulty lobbies unless you know you can handle it)

Important thing to note: The GPU I am using is on the lower end of hardware that is required to run Deep Rock. I have my game settings at low graphics and slightly reduced resolution and it's great, but weaker hardware is not advised!

review:
Most balanced game I've ever played. With a bit of skill you can meet every goal - doesn't matter which dwarf you play. It doesn't have raytracing, it doesn't need Lumen, and it reflects in the performance figures, which are great, even with a lower-specced PC. UE4 be praised! That's a big plus for me personally.

Modding is natively supported and there is an in-game mod browser - so there's no risk of f-ing up your config with a wrongly placed folder or file. Just make sure the mod versions you use are the same as the game version.

Go for it. It's just a really good game. Oh, and by the way, it's even more fun in co-op! ROCK AND STONE! [strike] don't forget about the beer [/strike]

review:
Смысл этой игры простой - уничтожать жуков, копать тоннели, собирать ресурсы и иногда выполнять специфические внутриигровые условности, после чего смотреть на прогрессию цифр и разблокировать аксессуары для персонажа. Здесь хороший дизайн, приятный саундтрек, но главное - у этой игры сформировалось дружелюбное комьюнити, регулярно генерирующее смешные картинки, фан арты и видео-гайды в интернетах.
Игра совершенно не воспринимается серьёзно благодаря своей меметичности, а соревновательный элемент здесь ограничен только финальными результатами задания, заключающимися в количестве добытых ресурсов и убитых жуков, из-за чего при игре в кооперативе здесь практически невозможно разругаться с напарниками, даже если один из них во время прохождения локации ни разу не выпустил кружку пива из руки. Периодически наведываясь в посвящённые игре чаты, вы запросто сможете найти себе там новых друзей или просто приятных собеседников на вечер.
Помимо социальной стороны этой игры, здесь так же есть возможность реализовать своё свободное время и для дворфов-одиночек. Взамен отсутствующих тиммейтов, игра выдаст вам летающего робота-напарника Боско, который будет помогать вам по мере своих сил и не даст расплакаться от гнетущего одиночества.
Несмотря на то что я поиграл столь мало часов в эту прекрасную игру, она успела оставить в моей душе неизгладимый след. Каждая минута в этом мире была наполнена особым смыслом: продуманный до мелочей геймплей, атмосферное музыкальное сопровождение и трогательная история.

Знаете, есть в этой игре что-то душевное. Не могу сказать конкретно за счёт чего этот эффект был достигнут, но в Deep Rock Galactic просто приятно находиться. Таких игр не так много в последнее время, так что не проходите мимо - как минимум на пару вечеров эта игра может скрасить ваше времяпрепровождение, а возможно она понравится вам настолько, что никаких других игр вам уже будет и не надо.

review_english: The point of this game is simple - destroy bugs, dig tunnels, collect resources and sometimes fulfill specific in-game conventions, then look at the progression of numbers and unlock accessories for the character. There is a good design, a pleasant soundtrack, but the main thing is that this game has a friendly community that regularly generates funny pictures, fan art and video guides on the Internet. The game is not taken seriously at all due to its memetic nature, and the competitive element here is limited only by the final results of the task, consisting in the number of resources extracted and beetles killed, which is why when playing in a cooperative game here it is almost impossible to quarrel with your partners, even if one of them never let go of a mug of beer during the passage of the location. By periodically visiting chats dedicated to the game, you can easily find new friends there or just pleasant interlocutors for the evening. In addition to the social side of this game, there is also an opportunity for single dwarves to use their free time. In return for missing teammates, the game will give you a flying robot partner, Bosco, who will help you to the best of his ability and will not let you cry from oppressive loneliness. Despite the fact that I played so few hours of this wonderful game, it managed to leave an indelible mark on my soul. Every minute in this world was filled with special meaning - gameplay thought out to the smallest detail, atmospheric music and a touching story. You know, there is something soulful in this game. I can’t say specifically how this effect was achieved, but it’s just a pleasure to be in Deep Rock Galactic. There aren’t many such games lately, so don’t pass by - at least for a couple of evenings this game can brighten up your time, and perhaps you’ll like it so much that you won’t need any other games.

review:
Let me get the one negative thing about this game out of the way first. Would dwarves be jumping around everywhere to avoid getting hit by bugs or would they dig a bunker, funnel the bugs through a choke, and kill them efficiently? In higher difficulties you forego immersing yourself into the role of a dwarf and start sprinting and jumping around everywhere to survive and that just totally takes me out of it and back into sweating on fortnite.

What are the positives about this game? LITERALLY EVERYTHING ELSE. 
-No aggressive monetization
-No fear of missing out on limited time gameplay and cosmetics
-No RTX5090 levels of recommended hardware, quite the opposite. The game runs VERY well
-Play the game your own way with your own class and your own build at your own difficulty
-Slow and steady pacing of upgrade and overclock unlocks allowing you to enjoy the gameplay for longer
-Your own skills at navigating caves and the different classes improves while you unlock upgrades
-Teamwork is strongly encouraged by the different classes fulfilling different roles
-Forced teamwork leads to brotherhood and no one ever crashes out
-Solo play is still fun at the right difficulty with an AI companion
-Beautiful visuals when you have a calm moment, varied biomes and cave layouts

I could gush about this game probably a bit more than this list but I wouldn't need any more convincing. Don't miss out on this game, I will look back on this as part of the Golden Age of Co-op game resurgence.

review:
Чудовий кооперативний шутер, в якому вам потрібно відбиватися від навали комах, виконувати різні місії та копати мінерали. До речі, копати тут приємно та особисто мене заспокоює. Ну і звісно, пити пиво та танцювати після успішно виконаного доручення. 

Підходить для посиденьок з друзями або просто гри з рандомами. Соло воїнам тут також раді. У грі є 4 унікальні класи, які додають різноманітності геймплею та командної взаємодії. Велика кількість білдів та оверклоків на зброю не дадуть вам занудьгувати. Також, у грі доволі просунута система кастомізації зовнішнього вигляду персонажа і різні скіни на зброю. 

Гра має приємну графіку. Оптимізація присутня. Гра не лагає і піде на будь-якому тостері. 

Тішить, що гру не закидають, та час від часу оновлюють. Постійні івенти на свята і DLC зі скінами. Гра часто продається зі знижкою.

Загалом, DRG залишає після себе приємні відчуття та бажання повернутися. З великим задоволенням раджу ознайомитися та сподіваюся, що ви тут залишитеся надовго.

12 ROCK AND STONE з 10

review_english: A great co-op shooter in which you have to fend off an onslaught of insects, complete various missions and dig for minerals. By the way, it's nice to dig here and personally it calms me down. And of course, drinking beer and dancing after a successfully completed assignment. Suitable for gatherings with friends or just playing with randoms. Solo warriors are also welcome here. The game has 4 unique classes that add variety to the gameplay and team interaction. A large number of images and overclocks for weapons will not let you get bored. Also, the game has a fairly advanced system for customizing the character's appearance and various weapon skins. The game has nice graphics. Optimization is present. The game does not lag and will run on any toaster. I am glad that the game is not abandoned, and is updated from time to time. Permanent events for holidays and DLC with skins. The game is often sold at a discount. Overall, DRG leaves behind a pleasant feeling and a desire to return. It is with great pleasure that I advise you to familiarize yourself with it and I hope that you will stay here for a long time. 12 ROCK AND STONE out of 10

review:
Зануритись в Донбаські печери з пивом та гномами - що ще може бути краще? Гра важить 3 гігабайти, а графіка в ній дуже гарна та приємна. Можна навіть сказати, що ця гра як Left 4 Dead, але не її копія, що радує.
Гарна та обширна кастомізація, а саме головне, чого я ще не бачив, - це змінний батл пас. Тобто я можу зайти в гру і через рік, і далі фармити той БП, який я обрав.
З друзями цікавіше, але і в соло можна спокійно грати без великих проблем. Особисто для себе в соло-грі обрав Бурильника, щоб швидко копати тунелі, і це ахуєєєєєєєєєєно.
Тягніть своїх бовдурів в цю гру - розібратись треба хвилин 15, а кайфу та сміху тут буде багато.

Але мушу зняти 1 бал за відсутність Львівського пива, тож 9/10. Рекомендую.

review_english: Immerse yourself in the Donbas caves with beer and gnomes - what could be better? The game weighs 3 gigabytes, and the graphics in it are very beautiful and pleasant. You can even say that this game is like Left 4 Dead, but not a copy of it, which is gratifying. Nice and extensive customization, the main one that I haven't seen yet is a changeable battle pass. That is, I can enter the game a year later and continue to farm the BP I chose. It's more interesting with friends, but you can also play solo without any major problems. Personally, in a solo game, I chose the Driller to quickly dig tunnels, and it's ahueeeeeeeeeeeeee. Drag your fools into this game - it takes 15 minutes to figure it out, and there will be a lot of fun and laughter here. But I have to remove 1 point for the lack of Lviv beer, so 9 out of 10. I recommend it.

review:
Rock and Stone Brothers!

Minerar? Sim.

Beber cerveja? Sim.

Odiar elfos? Com certeza.

Gritar com cogumelos? Essencial.

Entrei para minerar ouro, saí traumatizado por uma aranha gigante explosiva enquanto meu amigo me acertava com uma C4 'por querer'. Bebi uma cerveja que me fez flutuar, chutei barris na estação espacial e o Gerenciamento gritou comigo.

Se você tem amigos, esse jogo é 10/10. Se não tem, a comunidade vai te adotar em 5 minutos.

Se você não der o grito de guerra no lobby, você está jogando errado. O melhor simulador de trabalho escravo espacial com anões bêbados que você vai encontrar.

10/10 - Pelo Karl!

review:
Честно говоря, подустал от этой игры. Графика сначала нравилась, но быстро надоедает — всё одно и то же визуально. Стиль прикольный, но это не спасает.

Геймплей — главная проблема. В первое время весело, но потом становится скучно. Очень скучно. Миссии похожи одна на другую: зайди в пещеру, собери руду, убей пауков, повтори сто раз. Разнообразия почти нет. Разработчики добавляют контент медленно, а ждать надоедает.

Пиво в баре — забавно пару раз, потом просто фишка, которая не спасает от однообразия.

Лор? Да какой там лор. Он есть, но он никакой. Наброски, не больше.

Я понимаю, что игра сделана для компании с друзьями. Но если играть одному или даже в рандомной команде — быстро выгораешь.

Короче, если хотите игру, которая не надоест через 20-30 часов — проходите мимо. Deep Rock Galactic это не про долгоиграющую ценность. Наиграл своё и всё. Дальше просто бег по кругу без смысла.

P.S. 20-30 часов так как через это время ну прокачаешь пару дворфов на бронзу и устанешь от этого ведь понимаешь что в будущем тоже самое
я не из тех людей кто любит собирать шапки скины и тд

review_english: To be honest, I'm tired of this game. At first I liked the graphics, but I quickly got bored with the same visuals. The style is cool, but that doesn't help. Gameplay is the main problem. It's fun at first, but then it gets boring. Very boring. The missions are similar to one another: go into the cave, collect ore, kill spiders, repeat a hundred times. There is almost no variety. Developers add content slowly, and waiting gets annoying. Beer in a bar is funny a couple of times, then it’s just a feature that doesn’t save you from the monotony. Lor? What kind of lore is there? It exists, but it is nothing. Sketches, nothing more. I understand that the game is made for company with friends. But if you play alone or even in a random team, you quickly burn out. In short, if you want a game that won’t get boring after 20-30 hours, pass it by. Deep Rock Galactic is not about long-term value. I played mine and that’s it. Then you just run in circles without meaning. P.S. 20-30 hours because after this time you’ll level up a couple of dwarves to bronze and you’ll get tired of it because you understand that in the future the same thing I’m not one of those people who likes to collect hats, skins, etc.

review:
低予算ローポリアセットゲームみたいな見た目してるから心配になるけどこれは買っても良いやつ
採掘会社のドワーフになって採掘や脅威の排除をしよう！

ええ所が満載
・地形がランダム生成
およそのパターン(狭めの通路系、広間系など)はあるがその時その時、違うマップなので単調さが軽減されている

・武器改造OK
レベルが上がると武器改造で強くなる。ミッション中に拾える資源やミッション完了時にもらえるカネで強化できるのでメインミッションラインの攻略以外にミッションをやる意味がある

・難易度設定あり
難易度設定が可能で、敵の数や固さなどが上がるっぽい
ミッション報酬にも倍率がかかるのでやり応えがある

・世界観がおもしろい
フレンドリーファイアで罵り合うキャラクターども、
ミッション失敗後に服装が患者のガウンになる、
酒でバフがかかる、死んだ味方の蘇生も酒でやる、
などなど、絵にかいたようなコーヒーステインスタジオ製品

・公式にMOD対応
おかげでHPが数字で表示されるようになったり洞窟内の視認性を上げたりということが簡単にできる。
ただしMODありにすると経験値取得が無効になったりするのであしからず

髪型やおヒゲのカスタマイズ、武器スキンなど
コスメティック要素もあるので好きな人はその辺もハマるかも？
オレさまはむさいおっさんに興味ないからやらないけど

あかん所
・画質がよくない
ローポリモデルにテクスチャーもぺったりしてる。
こういうゲームちょこちょこ見るから好きな人は好きなんだろうけど
技術あるだろうから普通にきれいに作ってほしかった

review_english: I'm worried because it looks like a low-budget low-poly asset game, but this is a good buy. Become a dwarf at a mining company and do mining and eliminate threats. It's full of places. The terrain is randomly generated. There are general patterns (narrow passages, halls, etc.), but the map is different at that time, so it's less monotonous. Weapon modification is OK. As you level up, you can get stronger by modifying your weapons. You can strengthen yourself with the resources you pick up during a mission or the money you get when completing a mission, so there's a reason to do missions other than completing the main mission line.There is a difficulty setting.You can set the difficulty level, which seems to increase the number and hardness of enemies.Mission rewards are also multiplied, so it's rewarding.The worldview is interesting.Characters cursing each other with friendly fire.After a mission fails, the clothes change to a patient's gown.Buffs are given with alcohol.Dead allies can be revived with alcohol, etc. A picture-perfect coffee stain studio product. Officially mod-compatible. Thanks to this, you can easily display your HP as a number and increase visibility in caves. However, if you use mods, experience points acquisition will be disabled, so don't worry. There are also cosmetic elements such as hairstyle and beard customization, weapon skins, etc., so if you like it, you might like it. I'm not interested in ugly old men, so I won't play it, but it's okay. The image quality is not good.The textures are flat on the low poly model.I see games like this all the time, so I'm sure people who like it will like it, but I'm sure they have the skills to do it, so I wish they'd made it nice and clean.

review:
Deep Rock Galactic is objectively a good game and a lot of people really like it, but I'm not one of those people.
The weapons are all distinct and useful in their own ways. However, they don't seem to have my favorite class of weapon: a generic machine gun.
The mission objectives are all distinct with unique mechanics. But, I don't personally like the gameplay loop of running around the whole map trying to figure out where in the world the resonance crystals are.
None of these issues alone are enough to get me to post a negative review, instead just making the game not fun such that I don't play it. I specifically decided to post a negative review today because the process of trying to get Deep Rock Galactic to switch which monitor it's on was rather annoying and far more complicated than it needed to be.

review:
I picked this game up after playing the top down game, Survival on my Steam Deck. 

This game is hilarious fun solo, and gets better multiplayer. I haven't gotten too deep (see what I did there) into it, but have heard there are some very competitive players out there for this game. I play it with friends I have been gaming with for years, and there is a level of seriousness, but the game doesn't take itself too seriously.

DLC's are cosmetic items, no pay-to-win here. You can replay any event, so no FOMO pushed. No season pass to pay for, just play and have fun at your leisure. Currently on sale, (assuming for it's anniversary) I highly recommend it. Even at full price, it is still a great buy. Any DLC's are just icing on the cake to make your dwarf look like a badass.

review:
«Топор и пиво: братство под камнем»

Слушай. Есть путь мечника-одиночки — чистая пустота, ветер в спине, ни друга, ни врага. А есть путь рудокопа. Он грязнее. Но честнее.

Четверо в железных коробках падают сквозь тьму. Их оружие — не клинок, а сверло. Их враг — не демон, а камень. Пещеры Хоксеса не прощают ошибок: здесь нет дерева для медитации, нет тихой воды. Есть только взрыв, жвала и желтая метка.

И вот что странное, ученик. Каждый из них мог быть один. Один гном с киркой — труп. Четверо гномов с кирками — команда. Но не потому, что они сильнее. А потому, что когда один падает в пропасть, остальные плюют на руду и лезут вниз. Бесплатно.

Великое сожаление рудокопа — не в потерянной нитре. Не в сгоревшей миссии. Оно в том, что слишком поздно понимаешь: настоящая добыча — это не магнит и не золото. А тот момент, когда ты льешь пиво на могилу брата, который прикрыл тебя от личинки.

В глубоких скалах нет «я». Есть «мы». Даже корпорация «Глубинная Рок-Галактика» — лишь повод. Настоящая игра — научиться доверять тому, кто плюется кислотой в микрофон и называет тебя «бородатым червяком».

review_english: Ax and beer brotherhood under a stone Listen. There is the path of a lone swordsman, pure emptiness, the wind at your back, neither friend nor foe. And there is the miner's path. It's dirtier. But more honest. Four people in iron boxes fall through the darkness. Their weapon is not a blade, but a drill. Their enemy is not a demon, but a stone. The caves of Hoxes are unforgiving; there is no tree for meditation, no quiet water. There is only an explosion, a mandible and a yellow mark. And here’s the strange thing, student. Each of them could be alone. One gnome with a pickaxe is dead. Four dwarves with pickaxes team. But not because they are stronger. But because when one falls into the abyss, the rest spit on the ore and climb down. For free. The miner's great regret is not the lost nitrate. Not in the burned mission. The point is that you realize too late that real production is not a magnet or gold. And that moment when you pour beer on the grave of your brother, who protected you from the larvae. There is no I in the deep rocks. There we are. Even the Deep Rock Galaxy Corporation is just an excuse. The real game is learning to trust someone who spits acid into a microphone and calls you a bearded worm.

review:
If you are reading this you probably have some idea of what the gameplay is, so I'd like to focus on something different. The developers, Ghost Ship Games. GSG handles this game in the best possible way. Somehow they have implemented a "battle pass" in a good and non-predatory way. First of all, it's 100% free which is already great. Second, there are two passes, one focused on weapon skins, and the other on cosmetics and other things (and both are free!). Third, the weapon skin pass gives you a ton of control of the path you take, so instead of wasting your time unlocking skins you don't want, you can just get the one you do want! Fourth, NO FOMO! This applies to basically everything except a few hats related to early access or anniversaries but nothing in the battle passes are or most seasonal events are exclusive.  You can go back and play old seasons (including the events) for free! Plus, seasonal content comes back for it's respective events each year so you aren't missing out. Back to the battle passes. Fifth, the cosmetics and especially  the weapon skins are extremely high quality and adhere to the style of the game very well. As someone who can be very critical of cosmetics in games and how they fit in to the wider style of the game, I really have no critiques except for some of the seasonal stuff, but even that makes sense and is passible. All together you get a free user-friendly high quality battle pass, that you can progress through faster by playing the new content which is usually also very fun! GSG has done a great job handling microtransactions too. There are technically supporter packs and other cosmetics dlc's but you probably wouldn't even know that if you didn't look for them because GSG doesn't shove them in your face at all.  If you are looking to buy the dlc go ahead, it is also very high quality just like the free stuff. All of this in mind, there has to be a catch, right? Not really, the only minor think is have infrequently these updates come out, but that is more than ok because the updates are worth the wait. 

If you haven't played DRG you are missing out, because all of this and the gameplay is  awesome,
Rock and Stone!

review:
[h1]Deep Rock Galactic[/h1]
[h3]Amazing Co-op FPS made with Love[/h3]
[h3]Really worth trying![/h3]

Deep Rock Galactic is a wonderful project created by passionate developers for a niche audience that eventually grew into a massive and loyal community. Honestly, this is one of the most fun games I’ve played with friends in a very long time. 

[h3]Gameplay[/h3]
This title places you and three other players in a team of dwarves that must explore dangerous caves to complete objectives, collect resources, and survive endless alien hordes. It blends RPG elements such as character progression, unlockable upgrades, customizable weapons, classes, and different equipment choices. In this game, each class features unique weapons, tools and abilities to fulfill different purposes.

The mission variety in this game is great, with each one featuring its own objectives, mechanics, and obstacles. Fortunately, you can choose which mission to play at any given moment, as they constantly rotate through the game’s level system. 

The aiming system and the weapons feel very smooth and enjoyable. It is genuinely fun to experiment with different combinations of primary weapons and sidearms. This simple system adds a considerable amount of replayability to the game, especially when combined with its procedurally generated maps and random missions.

[h3]Art and World Design[/h3]
Deep Rock Galactic maintains a very stylized low-poly visual style with subtle cel-shading effects and rich environments. The atmosphere of Hoxxes, the planet explored throughout the game, remains mysterious and intriguing at all times. Each map creates a constant sense of tension and uncertainty through its alien vegetation, hostile wildlife, and its unpredictable cave systems.

On the other hand, I’m genuinely surprised by the lore of this game. It is far deeper than it initially appears. Much of the game’s lore, including the nature of its enemies and environments, is slowly uncovered through small details scattered across codex entries, weapon descriptions, and pieces of dialogue.

[h3]Music and Sound effects[/h3]
The game’s sound design is quite remarkable, as it’s pretty original and does an excellent job of building tension and immersion throughout every mission. The soundtrack is quite enjoyable to listen to, although sometimes, the tracks tend to overlap due to the game’s dynamic music system.

The mission variety in this game is great, with each one featuring its own objectives, mechanics, and obstacles. Fortunately, you can choose which mission to play at any given moment, as they constantly rotate through the game s level system. The aiming system and the weapons feel very smooth and enjoyable. It is genuinely fun to experiment with different combinations of primary weapons and sidearms. This simple system adds a considerable amount of replayability to the game, especially when combined with its procedurally generated maps and random missions. h3 Art and World Design h3 Deep Rock Galactic maintains a very stylized low-poly visual style with subtle cel-shading effects and rich environments. The atmosphere of Hoxxes, the planet explored throughout the game, remains mysterious and intriguing at all times. Each map creates a constant sense of tension and uncertainty through its alien vegetation, hostile wildlife, and its unpredictable cave systems. On the other hand, I m genuinely surprised by the lore of this game. It is far deeper than it initially appears. Much of the game s lore, including the nature of its enemies and environments, is slowly uncovered through small details scattered across codex entries, weapon descriptions, and pieces of dialogue. h3 Music and Sound effects h3 The game s sound design is quite remarkable, as it s pretty original and does an excellent job of building tension and immersion throughout every mission. The soundtrack is quite enjoyable to listen to, although sometimes, the tracks tend to overlap due to the game s dynamic music system.

[h1] Deep Rock Galactic - яркий пример того, как нужно монетизировать игру и относится к игру. Подобного или примера лучше вы не найдете [/h1] 
Начнем с батлпассов. Они литературно бесплатные. Это даже не по схеме "Задонать на один, закрой его и покупай следующие бесплатно". Он просто доступен всем. Более того, одни из немногих и скорее всего первых разработчиков, которые сделали возможности проходить ВСЕ старые батлпассы если вы их пропустили или не закрыли полностью.
Вы можете тыкнуть пальцем и спросить за длц, но там чисто косметика. Более того без каких-либо нюансов. В некоторых играх тоже "Только косметика", но нюанс в том, что там бесплатной косметики либо очень мало, либо она говно, либо все вместе. 
И я думаю многие как и я покупают почти все длц, даже если у них косметики и так валом по 2 причинам: само собой получить еще и новую косметику, но главное - выразить благодарность за нормальное отношение к игроку.

review:
I'm writing a review right as Rogue Core is releasing. So, regarding the classic DRG, it's almost perfect.
- The only thing you missed is full customization of all equipment. We need more options for a more nuanced selection of cosmetics, for example, allowing armor to be assembled into several parts to choose from-gloves, pants, torso. Also, add customization for all equipment, not just weapons but also auxiliary items like the platform gun, driller gloves, etc. If you added pickaxe customization, make it visible on the character as it hangs on the belt. It would be great to do the same with weapons-make them visible on the character's backpack. So, that's part of what's easy to add, now let's move on to something more complex.
- Overclock menu balance. Firstly, it looks bad-one big list for all class types. Separating the menu by class would be much more convenient. Add the ability to exchange duplicate overclocks, as it's common for overclocks that drop after they've already been crafted to be used.
- Progression during missions. Since the host can kick any random player at any time, it's worth adding a gradual progression. For example, a mission lasts 20 minutes. If a player plays for 10 minutes, they'll receive exactly half of the mission's completion credit, not just for standing, but also for their activity, collected minerals, and kills. This way, there won't be any wasted sessions; the player will always be progressing.
And finally, I wanted to remind you that it's better to develop what brought you the most profit-classic DRG. It's better to develop it than to make copies, which are much worse. Don't waste your time.
